meta.name: Dinoguessr dinosaur dataset
meta.upstreamNotes: Facts are normalized from RESTasaurus/Wikipedia. Clue images are matched from DinosaurPictures.org to avoid isolated-bone and name-labeled Wikimedia source images.
meta.popularitySource: English Wikipedia recent pageviews + curated fallback
meta.popularityTiers: Easy is the top 16% by popularity score, Medium is the next 34%, Hard is the rest.

dinosaurs.name: Aardonyx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aardonyx (Afrikaans aard, "earth" + Greek onux, "nail, claw") is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Abdarainurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Abdarainurus (meaning "Abdrant Nuru tail" after the holotype locality) is a genus of titanosaur dinosaur from the Alagteeg Formation in Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Abditosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Abditosaurus (meaning "forgotten lizard") is an extinct genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian) Conques Formation (Tremp Group) of Catalonia, Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Abelisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Abelisaurus (; "Abel's lizard") is a genus of predatory abelisaurid theropod dinosaur which lived during the Late Cretaceous Period (Campanian) of what is now South America.

dinosaurs.name: Abrictosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Abrictosaurus (; "wakeful lizard") is a genus of heterodontosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Early Jurassic in what is now in parts of southern Africa such as Lesotho and South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Abrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Abrosaurus (; 'delicate lizard' from the Greek αβρος meaning 'delicate' or 'dainty' and σαυρος meaning 'lizard') is a genus of macronarian sauropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Period of what is now Asia.

dinosaurs.name: Acantholipan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Acantholipan is a genus of herbivorous nodosaurid dinosaur from Mexico from the early Santonian age of the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Acanthopholis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Acanthopholis (; meaning "spiny scales") is a genus of ankylosaurian dinosaur in the family Nodosauridae that lived during the Late Cretaceous Period of England.

dinosaurs.name: Achelousaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Achelousaurus  is a genus of centrosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous Period of what is now North America, about 77 to 74.8 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Acheroraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Acheroraptor is an extinct genus of dromaeosaurid theropod dinosaur known from the latest Maastrichtian Hell Creek Formation of Montana, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Achillesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Achillesaurus is a genus of alvarezsaurid theropod dinosaur from the Santonian-aged (Late Cretaceous) Bajo de la Carpa Formation of Rio Negro, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Achillobator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Achillobator ( ə-KIL-ə-BAY-tor; meaning "Achilles hero") is a genus of large dromaeosaurid theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period about 96 million to 89 million years ago in what is now the Bayan Shireh Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Acristavus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Acristavus (meaning "non-crested grandfather") is a genus of saurolophine dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Acrocanthosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Acrocanthosaurus ( AK-roh-KAN-thə-SOR-əs; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Acrotholus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Acrotholus (Greek for "highest dome"- akros meaning highest and tholos meaning dome) is an extinct genus of pachycephalosaurian dinosaur that lived during the Santonian of the late Cretaceous, in the Milk River Formation of Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Adamantisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Adamantisaurus ( AD-ə-MAN-tiss-OR-əs) is a poorly-known genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period of what is now South America.

dinosaurs.name: Adasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Adasaurus ( AH-də-SOR-əs; meaning "evil lizard") is a genus of dromaeosaurid dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous period about 70 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Adelolophus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Adelolophus (meaning "unknown crest") is a genus of lambeosaurine dinosaur (a crested "duck-bill") from Upper Cretaceous rocks in the U.S. state of Utah.

dinosaurs.name: Adeopapposaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Adeopapposaurus (meaning "far eating lizard", in reference to its long neck) is a genus of plateosaurian dinosaur from the Early Jurassic Cañón del Colorado Formation of San Juan, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Adratiklit
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Adratiklit (meaning "mountain lizard") is an extinct genus of stegosaurian dinosaur that lived on the supercontinent Gondwana during the Middle Jurassic (Bathonian age).

dinosaurs.name: Adynomosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Adynomosaurus is a genus of lambeosaurine dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of what is now Catalonia, Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Aegyptosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aegyptosaurus /iːˌdʒɪptoʊˈsɔːrəs/ (meaning 'Egypt's lizard') is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived in what is now Africa, around 95 million years ago, during the Late Cretaceous Period (Cenomanian faunal stage).

dinosaurs.name: Aeolosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aeolosaurus (/ˌiːoʊloʊˈsɔːrəs/; "Aeolus' lizard") is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period of what is now South America.

dinosaurs.name: Aepisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aepisaurus (/ˌiːpɪˈsɔːrəs/; derived from the Greek: αἰπεινός, aipeinós - 'lofty/high' and σαυρος, sauros - 'lizard', i.e.

dinosaurs.name: Aepyornithomimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aepyornithomimus (meaning "Aepyornis mimic") is a genus of ornithomimid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Djadochta Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Aerosteon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aerosteon is a genus of megaraptoran dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous period of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Aetonyx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aetonyx is a dubious genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Early Jurassic of southern Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Afromimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Afromimus (meaning "Africa mimic") is a genus of ceratosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Elrhaz Formation of Niger.

dinosaurs.name: Afrovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Afrovenator (/ˌæfroʊvɪˈneɪtər/; "African hunter") is a genus of megalosaurid theropod dinosaur from the Middle or Late Jurassic Tiourarén Formation and maybe the Irhazer II Formation of the Sahara region of Niger in western Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Agathaumas
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Agathaumas (/æɡəˈθɔːməs/; "great wonder") is a dubious genus of a large ceratopsid dinosaur that lived in Wyoming during the Late Cretaceous (late Maastrichtian stage, 66 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Agilisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Agilisaurus (/ˌædʒɪlɪˈsɔːrəs/; 'agile lizard') is a genus of ornithischian dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Period of what is now eastern Asia.

dinosaurs.name: Agujaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Agujaceratops (meaning "horned face from Aguja") is a genus of chasmosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) of western Texas.

dinosaurs.name: Ajkaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ajkaceratops (pronounced "oi-ka-sera-tops") is a genus of ornithischian dinosaur described in 2010.

dinosaurs.name: Ajnabia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ajnabia (meaning "stranger" or "foreigner") is a genus of lambeosaurine hadrosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian) of Morocco.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Ajnabia odysseus maxilla by Nick Longrich

dinosaurs.name: Akainacephalus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Akainacephalus (meaning "thorn head") is a monospecific genus of ankylosaurid dinosaur from southern Utah that lived during the Late Cretaceous (late Campanian, 76.26 Ma) in what is now the Horse Mountain Gryposaur Quarry of the Kaiparowits Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Alamosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Alamosaurus (/ˌæləmoʊˈsɔːrəs/;[3] meaning "Ojo Alamo lizard") is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaurs containing a single known species, Alamosaurus sanjuanensis, from the Maastrichtian age of the Late Cretaceous period in what is now southwestern North America.

dinosaurs.name: Alaskacephale
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Alaskacephale is an extinct genus of pachycephalosaurid, a group of dome-headed, herbivorous ornithischian dinosaurs, that lived during the Maastrichtian stage of the Late Cretaceous period in what is now northern Alaska.[1] The genus is one of the few known Arctic dinosaurs and was found in the Prince Creek Formation, which preserves a menagerie of fossils.

dinosaurs.name: Albalophosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Albalophosaurus (meaning 'white crest lizard') is a genus of marginocephalian ornithischian dinosaur that lived in Japan during the Early Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Albertaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Albertaceratops (meaning "Alberta horned face") is a genus of centrosaurine horned dinosaur from the middle Campanian-age Upper Cretaceous Oldman Formation of Alberta, Canada.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Albertadromeus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Albertadromeus is an extinct genus of orodromine thescelosaurid dinosaur known from the upper part of the Late Cretaceous Oldman Formation (middle Campanian stage) of Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Albertavenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Albertavenator (meaning "Alberta hunter")[1] is a genus of small-bodied troodontid theropod dinosaur that lived during the early Maastrichtian stage of the Late Cretaceous period, approximately 71 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Albertonykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Albertonykus (meaning "Alberta claw") is an extinct genus of alvarezsaurid dinosaur from the Maastrichtian-age (Upper Cretaceous) rocks of the Horseshoe Canyon Formation of Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Albertosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Albertosaurus (; meaning "Alberta lizard") is a genus of large tyrannosaurid theropod dinosaur that lived in northwestern North America during the early to middle Maastrichtian age of the Late Cretaceous period, about 71 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Albinykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Albinykus (meaning "Albin claw", after a term used by Mongolian shamans to describe light phenomena in the Gobi Desert) is a genus of alvarezsaurid dinosaur whose fossils have been found in the Late Cretaceous-aged (Santonian) Javkhlant Formation in the Gobi Desert of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Alcovasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Alcovasaurus is an extinct genus of herbivorous stegosaurian dinosaurs that lived in the Late Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Alectrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Alectrosaurus (; meaning "alone lizard") is a genus of tyrannosauroid theropod dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous period, about some 96 million years ago in what is now the Iren Dabasu Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Aletopelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aletopelta (; meaning 'wanderer shield') is a monospecific genus of basal ankylosaurid dinosaur from Southern California that lived during the Late Cretaceous (upper Campanian stage, 75.5 Ma) in what is now the Point Loma Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Algoasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Algoasaurus (; "Algoa Bay reptile") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Berriasian to early Valanginian ages) Upper Kirkwood Formation of Cape Province, South Africa, specifically near a town called Despatch.

dinosaurs.name: Alioramus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Alioramus (; meaning 'different branch') is a genus of tyrannosaurid theropod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous period of Asia.

dinosaurs.name: Allosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Allosaurus ( AL-o-SAWR-us) is a genus of theropod dinosaur that lived 155 to 145 million years ago during the Late Jurassic period (Kimmeridgian to late Tithonian ages).

dinosaurs.name: Almas
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Almas is a genus of troodontid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Alocodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Alocodon is a genus of ornithischian dinosaur known from multiple teeth from the Middle or Late Jurassic Cabaços Formation of Portugal, and also the Forest Marble and Chipping Norton Formations of England.

dinosaurs.name: Alpkarakush
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Alpkarakush is an extinct genus of metriacanthosaurid theropod dinosaurs from the Jurassic Balabansai Formation of Kyrgyzstan.

dinosaurs.name: Altirhinus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Altirhinus (; "high nose") is a genus of hadrosauroid ornithopod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous period of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Altispinax
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Altispinax (; "with high spines") is a genus of large predatory theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous period (Valanginian, 140 to 133 million years ago) of what is now the Wadhurst Clay Formation of East Sussex, England.

dinosaurs.name: Alvarezsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Alvarezsaurus (; "Don Gregorio Alvarez's lizard") is a genus of alvarezsaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous, living in Argentina approximately 86 - 83 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Alxasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Alxasaurus (; meaning "Alxa lizard") is a genus of therizinosauroid theropod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous (Albian age) Bayin-Gobi Formation of Inner Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Amargasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Amargasaurus (/əˌmɑːrɡəˈsɔːrəs/; "La Amarga lizard") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous epoch (129.4–122.46 mya) of what is now Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Amazonsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Amazonsaurus (/ˌæməzənˈsɔːrəs/ AM-ə-zən-SOR-əs, 'Amazon lizard') is a genus of diplodocoid sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Period of what is now South America.

dinosaurs.name: Ambopteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ambopteryx (meaning "both wing") is a genus of scansoriopterygid dinosaur from the Oxfordian stage of the Late Jurassic of China.

dinosaurs.name: Ampelognathus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ampelognathus (meaning "grapevine jaw") is an extinct genus of possibly rhabdodontomorph ornithopod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian) Lewisville Formation of Texas.

dinosaurs.name: Ampelosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ampelosaurus (/ˌæmpɪloʊˈsɔːrəs/ AM-pi-loh-SOR-əs; meaning "vine lizard") is a titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period of what is now France.

dinosaurs.name: Amphicoelias
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Amphicoelias (/ˌæmfɪˈsiːliəs/, meaning "biconcave", from the Greek ἀμφί, amphi: "on both sides", and κοῖλος, koilos: "hollow, concave") is a genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur that lived approximately 150 million years ago during the Tithonian (Late Jurassic Period) of what is now Colorado, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Amtocephale
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Amtocephale is a genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaur from early Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian-Coniacian stages) deposits of southern Gobi Desert, Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Amurosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Amurosaurus (/əˌmʊərəˈsɔːrəs/; "Amur lizard") is a genus of lambeosaurine hadrosaurid dinosaur found in Late Cretaceous (70 to 66 million years ago) deposits of what is now eastern Asia.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Amygdalodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Amygdalodon (/əmɪɡˈdælədɒn/; "almond tooth" for its almond shaped teeth) is an extinct genus of basal sauropod from the Early Jurassic of Argentina.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Type material of Amygdalodon patagonicus Cabrera. 1947

dinosaurs.name: Anabisetia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anabisetia (/ˌɑːnəbiːˈsɛtiə/ AH-nə-bee-SET-ee-ə) is a genus of ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period of Patagonia, South America.

dinosaurs.name: Anasazisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anasazisaurus (/ˌɑːnəˌsɑːziˈsɔːrəs/ AH-nə-SAH-zee-SOR-əs; "Anasazi lizard") is a genus of saurolophine hadrosaurid ("duckbill") ornithopod dinosaur that lived about 74 million years ago, in the Late Cretaceous Period.

dinosaurs.name: Anchiceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anchiceratops longirostris C.M.

dinosaurs.name: Anchiornis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anchiornis is a genus of small, four-winged paravian dinosaurs, with only one known species, the type species Anchiornis huxleyi, named for its similarity to modern birds.

dinosaurs.name: Anchisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anchisaurus is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur.[1] It lived during the Early Jurassic Period, and its fossils have been found in the red sandstone of the Upper Portland Formation, Northeastern United States, which was deposited from the Hettangian age into the Sinemurian age, between about 200 and 192 million years ago.[2] Until recently it was classed as a member of Prosauropoda.

dinosaurs.name: Andesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Andesaurus (/ˌændəˈsɔːrəs/ AN-də-SOR-əs; "Andes lizard") is a genus of basal titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur which existed during the middle of the Cretaceous Period in South America.

dinosaurs.name: Angolatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Angolatitan (meaning "Angolan giant") is a genus of titanosauriform sauropod dinosaur from the Upper Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Angulomastacator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Angulomastacator (meaning "bend chewer", in reference to both the shape of its upper jaw and to the Big Bend area of the Rio Grande, where the type specimen was found) is a genus of duck-billed dinosaur from the Campanian-aged (Late Cretaceous) Aguja Formation

dinosaurs.name: Anhuilong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anhuilong (meaning "Anhui dragon") is a genus of mamenchisaurid dinosaur known from the Hongqin Formation of Anhui province, China.

dinosaurs.name: Aniksosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aniksosaurus (meaning "spring lizard", from Modern Greek Άνοιξη, "Spring", referring to the fact it was found on 21 September 1995, the onset of Spring on the Southern Hemisphere) is a genus of avetheropod dinosaur from what is now Chubut Province, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Animantarx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Animantarx (/ænɪˈmæntɑːrks/ ann-i-MAN-tarks; meaning 'living citadel') is a genus of nodosaurid ankylosaurian dinosaur from the Early and Late Cretaceous of western North America.

dinosaurs.name: Ankylosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ankylosaurus[nb 1] is a genus of armored dinosaur.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Ankylosaur head - cast - Custer County Montana - Museum of the Rockies - 2013-07-08

dinosaurs.name: Anodontosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anodontosaurus is an extinct genus of ankylosaurid dinosaurs within the subfamily Ankylosaurinae.

dinosaurs.name: Anomalipes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anomalipes (meaning "unusual foot") is an extinct genus of caenagnathid oviraptorosaurian dinosaurs known from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian age) Wangshi Group of China.

dinosaurs.name: Anoplosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anoplosaurus (meaning "unarmored or unarmed lizard") is an extinct genus of herbivorous nodosaurid dinosaur, from the late Albian-age Lower Cretaceous Cambridge Greensand of Cambridgeshire, England.

dinosaurs.name: Anserimimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anserimimus (/ˌænsərɪˈmaɪməs/ AN-sər-im-EYE-məs; "goose mimic") is a genus of ornithomimid theropod dinosaur, from the Late Cretaceous Period of what is now Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Antarctopelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Antarctopelta (ann-TARK-toh-PEL-tə; meaning 'Antarctic shield') is a genus of ankylosaurian dinosaur, a group of large, quadrupedal herbivores, that lived during the Maastrichtian stage of the Late Cretaceous period on what is now James Ross Island, Antarctica.

dinosaurs.name: Antarctosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Antarctosaurus (; meaning "southern lizard") is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period of what is now South America.

dinosaurs.name: Anteavis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anteavis (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Antetonitrus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Antetonitrus is a genus of sauropodiform dinosaur found in the Early Jurassic Elliot Formation of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Antrodemus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Antrodemus ("chamber bodied") is a dubious genus of theropod dinosaur from the Upper Jurassic, probably the Morrison Formation, of Middle Park, Colorado.

dinosaurs.name: Anzu
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Anzu (named for Anzû, a bird-like daemon in Ancient Mesopotamian religion) is a genus of caenagnathid dinosaur from North Dakota, South Dakota, and Montana that lived during the Late Cretaceous (upper Maastrichtian stage, 67.2-66.0 Ma) in what is now the Hell Creek Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Aoniraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Cretaceous (late Cenomanian-early Turonian), ~96-91 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Aoniraptor is an extinct genus of megaraptoran theropod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous Huincul Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Aorun
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aorun  (敖闰 pinyin Áo rùn) is a genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur first discovered in 2006, with its scientific description published in 2013.

dinosaurs.name: Apatoraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Apatoraptor (meaning "deceptive thief") is a genus of caenagnathid dinosaur which contains a single species, A. pennatus.

dinosaurs.name: Apatosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Apatosaurus (; meaning "deceptive lizard") is a genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur that lived in North America during the Late Jurassic period.

dinosaurs.name: Appalachiosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Appalachiosaurus ( AP-ə-LAY-chee-oh-SOR-əs; "Appalachian lizard") is a genus of tyrannosauroid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous period of what is now eastern North America.

dinosaurs.name: Aquilarhinus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aquilarhinus (meaning "eagle snout" after the unusual beak morphology) is a genus of hadrosaurid ornithopod dinosaur from the Aguja Formation from Texas in the United States.

dinosaurs.name: Aquilops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aquilops is an early herbivorous ceratopsian dinosaur dating from the Early Cretaceous of North America, approximately 109 million to 104 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Arackar
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Arackar (meaning "skeleton" in Kunza) is an extinct genus of lithostrotian sauropod, possibly part of the Saltasauridae, discovered in the Hornitos Formation of Atacama Province, Chile.

dinosaurs.name: Aragosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aragosaurus (meaning "Aragon lizard") was a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous period of Galve, province of Teruel, in the autonomous territory of Aragón, Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Aralosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aralosaurus is a genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous in what is now Kazakhstan.

dinosaurs.name: Aratasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aratasaurus is an extinct genus of basal coelurosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Aptian) Romualdo Formation of Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Archaeoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Archaeoceratops, meaning "ancient horned face", is a genus of basal neoceratopsian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Aptian stage) of north central China.

dinosaurs.name: Archaeocursor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Archaeocursor (meaning "old runner") is an extinct genus of basal ornithischian dinosaurs from the Early Jurassic Ziliujing Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Archaeodontosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Archaeodontosaurus ("ancient-toothed lizard") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Archaeopteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Archaeopteryx (/ˌɑːrkiːˈɒptərɪks/ ⓘ; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Archaeornithoides
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Archaeornithoides is a genus of maniraptoran theropod dinosaur of the Late Cretaceous of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Archaeornithomimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Archaeornithomimus (meaning "ancient bird mimic") is a genus of ornithomimosaurian theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period (around 96 million years ago) in the Iren Dabasu Formation of Inner Mongolia, China.

dinosaurs.name: Arcovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Arcovenator ("Arc hunter") is an extinct genus of abelisaurid theropod dinosaurs hailing from the Late Cretaceous of France and possibly Spain.[1][2] The type and only described species is Arcovenator escotae.[1] Its name derives from the river Arc flowing nea

dinosaurs.name: Arcusaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Arcusaurus is an extinct genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Early Jurassic (Pliensbachian stage) of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Ardetosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ardetosaurus is an extinct genus of diplodocid sauropod dinosaurs from the Late Jurassic (Kimmeridgian) Morrison Formation of northern Wyoming, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Arenysaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Arenysaurus is a genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (66 million years ago), being one of the last non-avian dinosaurs and it went extinct during the Cretaceous–Paleogene extinction event.

dinosaurs.name: Argentinosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Argentinosaurus (meaning "lizard from Argentina") is a genus of giant sauropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period in what is now Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Aristosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aristosaurus is a dubious genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Early Jurassic of southern Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Aristosuchus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aristosuchus (from the Ancient Greek ἄριστος, meaning "bravest, best, noblest", and σουχος, the Ancient Greek corruption of the name of the Egyptian crocodile-headed god Sobek) is a genus of small coelurosaurian dinosaur that lived in the Early Cretaceous period (Barremian stage, sometime between 130 and 123 million years ago) of what is now England, UK.

dinosaurs.name: Arkansaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Arkansaurus (meaning "Arkansas lizard") is an extinct genus of ornithomimosaurian theropod dinosaur that lived during the Aptian and Albian stages of the Early Cretaceous in what is now North America.

dinosaurs.name: Arkharavia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Arkharavia (meaning "Arkhara road") is a dubious genus of somphospondylan sauropod, although at least some of its remains probably belong to a hadrosaurid.[1] It was discovered in the Udurchukan Formation in Russia and lived during the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Arrhinoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Arrhinoceratops (meaning "no nose-horn face", derived from the Ancient Greek "a-/α-" "no", rhis/ῥίς "nose" "keras/κέρας" "horn", "-ops/ὤψ" "face") is a genus of herbivorous ceratopsian dinosaur.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Arrhinoceratops brachyops skull, Near Drumheller, Alberta, Late Cretaceous - Royal Ontario Museum - DSC00076

dinosaurs.name: Arrudatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Arrudatitan (meaning "Arruda's giant") is an extinct genus of titanosaur sauropod dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian-Maastrichtian)-aged Adamantina Formation of Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Arstanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Arstanosaurus (meaning "Arstan lizard" after the Arstan well) is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous-aged (Santonian-Campanian) Bostobe Formation of Kazakhstan.

dinosaurs.name: Asfaltovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Asfaltovenator (meaning "Cañadón Asfalto Formation hunter" after the fossil formation in which its fossils were found) is a genus of possibly allosauroid dinosaur from the Lower Jurassic (Middle Toarcian) Cañadón Asfalto Formation of Chubut Province, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Asiaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Asiaceratops (meaning "Asian horned face") is a genus of herbivorous ceratopsian dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Asiatosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Asiatosaurus (meaning "Asian lizard") is an extinct genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur which lived during the Early Cretaceous in Mongolia and China.[1] The type species is known only from teeth, making it difficult to rely on information until more specimens are found to expand our knowledge, and another species is known, also based on scant remains; both are now classified as nomina dubia.

dinosaurs.name: Asiatyrannus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Asiatyrannus (meaning "Asian tyrant") is an extinct genus of tyrannosaurine theropod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous Nanxiong Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Astrodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Astrodon is a genus of large herbivorous sauropod dinosaur, measuring 20 m (66 ft) in length, 9 m (30 ft) in height and 20 metric tons (22 short tons) in body mass.[2][3][4] It lived in what is now the eastern United States during the Early Cretaceous period,

dinosaurs.name: Astrophocaudia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Early Cretaceous, Early Albian, 112.2-106 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Astrophocaudia is an extinct genus of somphospondylan sauropod known from the later part of the Early Cretaceous (Albian stage) of Texas, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Asylosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Asylosaurus (meaning "unharmed or sanctuary lizard") is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Late Triassic Avon Fissure Fill of England.

dinosaurs.name: Atacamatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Atacamatitan (meaning "Atacama Desert titan") is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous Tolar Formation of Chile.

dinosaurs.name: Athenar
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Athenar is an extinct genus of dicraeosaurid sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic of Utah, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Atlantosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Atlantosaurus (meaning "Atlas lizard") is a dubious genus of sauropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Atlasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Atlasaurus is a genus of sauropod dinosaurs from Middle Jurassic (Bathonian to Callovian stages) beds in North Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Atlascopcosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Atlascopcosaurus  is a genus of herbivorous basal iguanodont dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Eumeralla Formation of Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Atrociraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian), 72.2-71.5 Ma Possibly lived to 68 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Atrociraptor  is a genus of dromaeosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous in what is now Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Atsinganosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Atsinganosaurus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur which existed in what is now France during the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Aublysodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aublysodon (“backwards-flowing tooth") is a dubious genus of carnivorous dinosaurs known only from the Judith River Formation in Montana, which has been dated to the late Campanian age of the late Cretaceous period (about 75 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Aucasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aucasaurus (meaning 'Auca Mahuevo lizard') is a genus of medium-sized abelisaurid theropod dinosaur from Argentina that lived during the Late Cretaceous (Santonian to Campanian stage) of the Anacleto Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Augustynolophus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Augustynolophus is an extinct genus of herbivorous saurolophine hadrosaur dinosaur which was discovered in the Moreno Formation in California, dating to the late Maastrichtian age, making it one of the last dinosaurs known from the fossil record before the Cre

dinosaurs.name: Auroraceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Auroraceratops, meaning "dawn horned face", is a genus of bipedal basal neoceratopsian dinosaur, from the Early Cretaceous (Aptian age) of north central China.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Discovery Site Simulation of Auroraceratops rugosus

dinosaurs.name: Aurornis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aurornis is an extinct genus of anchiornithid theropod dinosaurs from the Jurassic period of China.

dinosaurs.name: Australodocus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Australodocus (meaning "southern beam" from the Latin australis "southern" and the Greek dokos/δοκоς "beam") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Jurassic period, around 150 million years ago, in what is now Lindi Region, Tanzania.

dinosaurs.name: Australotitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Australotitan  is an extinct genus of possibly titanosaurian somphospondylan dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous Winton Formation (Cenomanian–Turonian) of southern-central Queensland, Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Australovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Australovenator (meaning "southern hunter") is a genus of megaraptoran theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Winton Formation (Cenomanian, 95 million years ago) of Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Austrocheirus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Austrocheirus is an extinct genus of theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Austroposeidon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Austroposeidon is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Presidente Prudente Formation of Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Austroraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Austroraptor ( AW-stroh-RAP-tər) is a genus of unenlagiine theropod dinosaur that lived during the Campanian and Maastrichtian ages of the Late Cretaceous period in what is now Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Austrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Austrosaurus (/ˌɔːstroʊˈsɔːrəs/; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Avaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Avaceratops is a genus of small herbivorous ceratopsian dinosaurs which lived during the late Campanian in what are now the Northwest United States.

dinosaurs.name: Aviatyrannis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Aviatyrannis is a genus of coelurosaurian theropod dinosaur, either a tyrannosauroid or an ornithomimosaurian, from the Oxfordian-Tithonian stages of the Late Jurassic found in Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Avimimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Avimimus (/ˌeɪvɪˈmaɪməs/ AY-vim-EYE-məs), meaning "bird mimic" (Latin avis = bird + mimus = mimic), is a genus of oviraptorosaurian theropod dinosaur, named for its bird-like characteristics, that lived in the late Cretaceous in what is now Mongolia, around 85

dinosaurs.name: Avisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Avisaurus (meaning "bird lizard") is a genus of enantiornithine avialan from the Late Cretaceous of North America.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Baalsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Baalsaurus (named after the dinosaur fossil site Baal in Argentina, which in turn is named after the ancient Phoenician god Baal) is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Neuquén Province, Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Bactrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bactrosaurus (/ˌbæktrəˈsɔːrəs/; meaning "Club lizard," "baktron" = club + sauros = lizard) is a genus of herbivorous hadrosauroid dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous, from about 96 to 85 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Bagaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bagaceratops (meaning "small-horned face") is a genus of small protoceratopsid dinosaurs that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous, around 72 to 71 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Bagaraatan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bagaraatan (/'ba-ɣa-raa-tan/ meaning 'small' baɣa + 'carnivorous animal, beast of prey' araatan in Mongolian) is a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous period of what is now the Nemegt Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Bagualia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bagualia (meaning "wild horse") is an extinct genus of eusauropod dinosaur from the Early Jurassic (middle Toarcian) Cañadón Asfalto Formation in what is now the Chubut Province of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Bahariasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bahariasaurus (meaning "Bahariya lizard") is an enigmatic genus of large theropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Bainoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bainoceratops (meaning "mountain horned face", after the type locality, Bayn Dzak) is a genus of ceratopsian dinosaur from the late Campanian in the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Baiyinosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Baiyinosaurus (meaning "Baiyin reptile") is an extinct genus of stegosaurian dinosaurs from the Middle Jurassic Wangjiashan Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Bajadasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bajadasaurus is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous epoch (late Berriasian to Valanginian stages, between 145 and 132.9 million years ago) of northern Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Bambiraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bambiraptor is a genus of bird-like dromaeosaurid theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous (about 72 million years ago) of Montana.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Dans l'ombre des dinosaures - Bambiraptor jeune - 04

dinosaurs.name: Banji
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Banji is an extinct genus of oviraptorid dinosaur that lived approximately 66 million years ago during the later part of the Cretaceous Period in what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Bannykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bannykus is an extinct genus of alvarezsauroid from the Early Cretaceous Bayin-Gobi Formation of Inner Mongolia, China.

dinosaurs.name: Baotianmansaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Baotianmansaurus is an extinct genus of somphospondylian sauropod.

dinosaurs.name: Barapasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Barapasaurus (/bəˌrɑːpəˈsɔːrəs/ bə-RAH-pə-SOR-əs) is a genus of basal sauropod dinosaur from Jurassic rocks of India.

dinosaurs.name: Barilium
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Barilium is a genus of iguanodontian dinosaur which was first described as a species of Iguanodon (I. dawsoni) by Richard Lydekker in 1888, the specific epithet honouring the discoverer Charles Dawson, who collected the holotype during the 1880s.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Barosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Barosaurus (/ˌbæroʊˈsɔːrəs/ BARR-oh-SOR-əs) is a genus of giant, long-tailed, long-necked, plant-eating sauropod dinosaur closely related to the more familiar Diplodocus.

dinosaurs.name: Barrosasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Barrosasaurus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur, first described by paleontologists Leonardo Salgado and Rodolfo Coria in 2009.

dinosaurs.name: Barsboldia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Barsboldia (meaning "of Barsbold", a well-known Mongolian paleontologist) is a genus of large hadrosaurid dinosaur from the early Maastrichtian Nemegt Formation of Ömnogöv, Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Baryonyx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Baryonyx (/ˌbæriˈɒnɪks/) is a genus of theropod dinosaur which lived in the Barremian stage of the Early Cretaceous period, about 130–125 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Bashanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bashanosaurus (meaning "Bashan lizard") is an extinct genus of stegosaurian dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic (Bathonian age) Shaximiao Formation of Yunyang County, China.

dinosaurs.name: Bashunosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bashunosaurus is a genus of potentially macronarian sauropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Shaximiao Formation of Kaijiang, China.

dinosaurs.name: Batyrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Batyrosaurus is an extinct genus of herbivorous basal hadrosauroid dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous Bostobe Formation (Santonian to Campanian stage) of central Kazakhstan.

dinosaurs.name: Baurutitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Baurutitan is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous in what is now Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Bayannurosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bayannurosaurus is a non-hadrosauriform ankylopollexian ornithopod described in 2018 by Xu Xing.

dinosaurs.name: Beibeilong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Beibeilong (Chinese: 贝贝龙, transl. baby dragon) is a genus of large caenagnathid dinosaurs that lived in China during the Late Cretaceous epoch, about 96 million to 88 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Beipiaosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Beipiaosaurus is a genus of therizinosauroid theropod dinosaurs that lived in China during the Early Cretaceous in the Yixian Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Beishanlong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Beishanlong is a genus of giant ornithomimosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of China.

dinosaurs.name: Bellusaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bellusaurus (meaning "Beautiful lizard", from Vulgar Latin bellus 'beautiful' (masculine form) and Ancient Greek sauros 'lizard') was a sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic (Oxfordian) known from juvenile specimens that would have measured about 4.8 metres (16 ft) long.

dinosaurs.name: Berberosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Berberosaurus (meaning "Berber lizard", in reference to the Berbers of Morocco) is a genus of neotheropod dinosaur, possibly a ceratosaur, from the Toarcian-age (Lower Jurassic) "Toundoute Continental Series" (Azilal Formation) found in the Central High Atlas of Toundoute, Ouarzazate, Morocco.

dinosaurs.name: Berthasaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Berthasaura (meaning "Bertha's lizard") is an extinct genus of ceratosaurian theropod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous Goio-Erê Formation of Paraná, Brazil.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Berthasaura leopoldinae 3d Reconstruction Alex Pritchard Dinosauria - Dinosaur Fossil Replicas

dinosaurs.name: Betasuchus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Betasuchus is a genus of probable abelisaurid theropod dinosaur which lived during the Late Cretaceous Period.

dinosaurs.name: Bicentenaria
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bicentenaria (meaning "two hundred year anniversary"; named after the 200th anniversary of the 1810 May Revolution in Argentina) is an extinct genus of carnivorous coelurosaurian theropods which lived during the early Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian) Candeleros Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Bienosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bienosaurus (meaning "Boien's lizard) is a dubious genus of thyreophoran dinosaur from the Early Jurassic (probably Hettangian to Sinemurian) Lower Lufeng Formation of Yunnan Province in China.

dinosaurs.name: Bistahieversor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bistahieversor (meaning "Bistahi destroyer"), also known as the "Bisti Beast", is a genus of basal eutyrannosaurian theropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Bisticeratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Cretaceous, late Campanian, 74 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Bisticeratops (meaning "Bisti/De-Na-Zin Wilderness horned face") is a genus of chasmosaurine ceratopsian from outcrops of the Campanian age Kirtland Formation found in the Bisti/De-Na-Zin Wilderness in northwestern New Mexico, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Blasisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Blasisaurus is a genus of lambeosaurine hadrosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Blikanasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Blikanasaurus is a genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur from the late Triassic of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Bolong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bolong (meaning "Bo's dragon") is a genus of iguanodontian dinosaur known from the Early Cretaceous Yixian Formation of western Liaoning Province, China.[1] It lived about 125 million years ago in the earliest Aptian.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Bonapartenykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Cretaceous, middle Campanian-lower Maastrichtian
dinosaurs.description: Bonapartenykus (meaning "José F.

dinosaurs.name: Bonapartesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bonapartesaurus (meaning "Bonaparte's lizard", named after José Bonaparte) is an extinct genus of herbivorous ornithopod dinosaur belonging to Hadrosauridae, which lived in the area of modern Argentina during the Campanian and Maastrichtian stages of the Late

dinosaurs.name: Bonatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bonatitan is a genus of titanosaurian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Allen Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Bonitasaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bonitasaura is a genus of titanosaurian dinosaur hailing from uppermost layers of the Late Cretaceous (Santonian) Bajo de la Carpa Formation, Neuquén Group of the eastern Neuquén Basin, located in Río Negro Province, Northwestern Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Borealopelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Borealopelta (meaning 'northern shield') is a genus of nodosaurid ankylosaur from the Lower Cretaceous of what is today Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Borealosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Borealosaurus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of northern China.

dinosaurs.name: Boreonykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Boreonykus is an extinct genus of dromaeosaurid dinosaur, that lived during the Late Cretaceous in the area of present Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Borogovia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Borogovia is a troodontid theropod dinosaur genus which lived during the Late Cretaceous Period, in what is now Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Bothriospondylus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bothriospondylus (from Ancient Greek βοθρίον (bothríon), meaning "trench", and σπόνδυλος (spóndulos), meaning "vertebra") is a dubious genus of possibly neosauropodan sauropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Brachiosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Brachiosaurus (/ˌbrækiəˈsɔːrəs/) is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived in North America during the Late Jurassic, about 155.6 to 145.5 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Brachyceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Brachyceratops ('short horned face') is a dubious genus of ceratopsian dinosaur from the late Cretaceous Period of Montana, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Brachylophosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Brachylophosaurus (/brəˌkɪləfəˈsɔːrəs/ brə-KIL-ə-fə-SOR-əs or /ˌbrækiˌloʊfəˈsɔːrəs/ brak-i-LOH-fə-SOR-əs) is a genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period of western North America.

dinosaurs.name: Brachytrachelopan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Brachytrachelopan is a genus of short-necked sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic (Oxfordian to Tithonian) of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Bradycneme
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bradycneme (meaning "ponderous leg") is a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Upper Cretaceous-aged (Maastrichtian) Sânpetru Formation of the Hațeg Basin, Transylvania, Romania.

dinosaurs.name: Brasilotitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Brasilotitan (meaning "Brazil giant") is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (early Maastrichtian) Adamantina Formation of Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Bravasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bravasaurus (meaning Laguna Brava lizard) is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Ciénaga del Río Huaco Formation of La Rioja, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Bravoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bravoceratops is a genus of large chasmosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur that lived approximately 70 million years ago, and is known from the Late Cretaceous Javelina Formation in what is now Texas, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Breviceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Breviceratops (meaning "short horned face") is a genus of protoceratopsid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous in what is now the Barun Goyot Formation, Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Brighstoneus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Brighstoneus (after Brighstone, a village on the Isle of Wight) is a genus of hadrosauriform dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Wessex Formation of the Isle of Wight, England.

dinosaurs.name: Brontomerus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Brontomerus (from Greek bronte meaning "thunder", and merós meaning "thigh") is a genus of camarasauromorph sauropod which lived during the early Cretaceous (Aptian or Albian age, approximately 110 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Brontosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Brontosaurus (/ˌbrɒntəˈsɔːrəs/;[1][2] meaning "thunder lizard" from the Greek words βροντή, brontē "thunder" and σαῦρος, sauros "lizard") is a genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur that lived in present-day United States during the Late Jurassic period.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Brontosaurus excelsus remount in the Peabody Museum

dinosaurs.name: Bruhathkayosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bruhathkayosaurus (/bruːˌhæθkeɪoʊˈsɔːrəs/; meaning "huge-bodied lizard") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur found in the Kallamedu Formation of India.

dinosaurs.name: Buitreraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Buitreraptor (meaning "La Buitrera seizer") is a genus of unenlagiine theropod dinosaurs that lived during the Late Cretaceous of Argentina at the Candeleros Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Burianosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Burianosaurus is an extinct genus of ornithopod dinosaur that lived during the Cenomanian stage of the Late Cretaceous period in what is now the Czech Republic, being the first validly named dinosaur from that country.

dinosaurs.name: Buriolestes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Buriolestes is a genus of early sauropodomorph dinosaurs from the Late Triassic Santa Maria Formation of the Paraná Basin in southern Brazil.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Foto do bloco com o buriolestes Foto Flavio Lopes (Ufrgs)

dinosaurs.name: Bustingorrytitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Bustingorrytitan (meaning "Bustingorry's giant") is a genus of lithostrotian titanosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian) Huincul Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Byronosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Byronosaurus is a genus of troodontid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Caenagnathasia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Caenagnathasia ('recent jaw from Asia') is a genus of small caenagnathid oviraptorosaurian theropod from the Late Cretaceous of Uzbekistan.

dinosaurs.name: Caenagnathus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Caenagnathus ('recent jaw') is a genus of caenagnathid oviraptorosaurian dinosaur from the late Cretaceous period (Campanian stage, about 75 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Caieiria
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Caieiria (named after the Caieira locality where its fossils were recovered) is a genus of titanosaurian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Serra da Galga Formation (formerly a unit of the Marília Formation) of Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Caihong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Caihong (Chinese: 彩虹; pinyin: cǎihóng; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Calamosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Calamosaurus (meaning "reed lizard") is a genus of small theropod dinosaur, possibly a tyrannosauroid, from the Early Cretaceous of the Isle of Wight, England.

dinosaurs.name: Caletodraco
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Caletodraco is a genus of furileusaurian abelisaurid dinosaur from Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian) Chalk of the Pays de Caux, France.

dinosaurs.name: Camarasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Camarasaurus ( KAM-ər-ə-SOR-əs) is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived in North America during the Late Jurassic epoch.

dinosaurs.name: Camarillasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Camarillasaurus (meaning "Camarillas lizard") is an extinct genus of spinosaurid theropod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous period (Barremian) of Camarillas, Teruel Province, in what is now northeastern Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Camposaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Triassic, middle Norian, 220 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Camposaurus ( KAMP-o-SOR-əs) is a coelophysid dinosaur genus from the Norian stage of the Late Triassic period of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Camptosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Camptosaurus ( KAMP-tə-SOR-əs) is a genus of plant-eating, beaked ornithischian dinosaurs of the Late Jurassic period of western North America and possibly also Europe.

dinosaurs.name: Campylodoniscus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Campylodoniscus is a genus of titanosaur sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period of what is now Argentina.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Campylodoniscus ameghinoi Skull Mk I Me

dinosaurs.name: Canardia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Canardia is an extinct genus of lambeosaurine dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous Marnes d'Auzas Formation (late Maastrichtian stage) of Haute-Garonne department, in Occitanie region, southwestern France.

dinosaurs.name: Carcharodontosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Carcharodontosaurus (/ˌkɑːrkəroʊˌdɒntoʊˈsɔːrəs/; "shark-toothed lizard") is a genus of large theropod dinosaur that lived in Northwest Africa from about 100 to 94 million years ago during the Cenomanian stage of the Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Cardiodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cardiodon (meaning "heart tooth", in reference to the shape) was a herbivorous genus of sauropod dinosaur, based on a tooth from the Middle Jurassic (late Bathonian) Forest Marble Formation of Wiltshire, England.

dinosaurs.name: Carnotaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Carnotaurus /ˌkɑːrnoʊˈtɔːrəs/ is a genus of theropod dinosaur that lived in South America during the Late Cretaceous period, between 69 and 66 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Cathartesaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cathartesaura is a genus of rebbachisaurid sauropod dinosaur from Late Cretaceous strata of the Huincul Formation, at the "La Buitrera" locality, in the Neuquén Basin of Río Negro Province, Argentina.[1] The fossil remains, described by Gallina and Apesteguía in 2005, consist of a partial skeleton including vertebrae and limb bones.

dinosaurs.name: Caudipteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Caudipteryx (meaning "tail feather") is a genus of small oviraptorosaurian dinosaurs that lived in Asia during the Early Cretaceous, around 124.6 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Cedarosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cedarosaurus (meaning "Cedar lizard" - named after the Cedar Mountain Formation, in which it was discovered) is a genus of nasal-crested macronarian sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Period (Valanginian).

dinosaurs.name: Cedarpelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cedarpelta is an extinct genus of basal ankylosaurid dinosaur from Utah that lived during the Late Cretaceous period (Cenomanian to lower Turonian stage, 98.2 to 93 Ma) in what is now the Mussentuchit Member of the Cedar Mountain Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Cedrorestes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cedrorestes is a genus of iguanodontian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of Utah.

dinosaurs.name: Centrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Centrosaurus (/ˌsɛntroʊˈsɔːrəs/ SEN-troh-SOR-əs; lit.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Centrosaurus apertus skull and jaws, Dinosaur Provincial Park, Alberta, Canada, Late Cretaceous - Royal Ontario Museum - DSC00078

dinosaurs.name: Cerasinops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cerasinops (meaning 'cherry face') was a small ceratopsian dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Ceratonykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ceratonykus (meaning "horned claw") is a monospecific genus of alvarezsaurid dinosaur from Mongolia that lived during the Late Cretaceous (late Campanian stage, 72.1 Ma) in what is now the Barun Goyot Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Ceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ceratops (meaning 'horn face') is a dubious genus of herbivorous ceratopsian dinosaur which lived during the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Ceratosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ceratosaurus /ˌsɛrətoʊˈsɔːrəs/ (from Greek κέρας / κέρατος keras / keratos 'horn' and σαῦρος sauros 'lizard') is a genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Jurassic period in North America and Europe.

dinosaurs.name: Ceratosuchops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ceratosuchops (meaning "horned crocodile face") is a genus of spinosaurid from the Early Cretaceous (Barremian) Wessex Formation of Britain.

dinosaurs.name: Cetiosauriscus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cetiosauriscus (/ˌsiːtioʊˈsɔːrɪskəs/ SEE-tee-oh-SOR-iss-kəs)[3] is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived between 166 and 164 million years ago during the Callovian (Middle Jurassic Period) in what is now England.

dinosaurs.name: Cetiosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cetiosaurus (/ˌsiːtioʊˈsɔːrəs, ˌsiːʃi-/[1] meaning 'whale lizard', from the Greek keteios/κήτειος meaning 'sea monster' (later, 'whale') and sauros/σαυρος meaning 'lizard'), is a genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Period, living ab

dinosaurs.name: Chadititan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chadititan (meaning "titan of the salt") is an extinct genus of titanosaurian dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) Anacleto Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Chakisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chakisaurus (meaning "elder guanaco lizard") is an extinct genus of elasmarian ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Huincul Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Changchunsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Changchunsaurus (meaning "Changchun lizard") is an extinct genus of small herbivorous neornithischian dinosaur from Early or Late Cretaceous deposits of Gongzhuling, Jilin, China.

dinosaurs.name: Changmiania
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Changmiania (from the Chinese "changmian", meaning "eternal sleep") is a genus of basal ornithopod dinosaur that lived in what is now China during the Early Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Changyuraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Changyuraptor is a genus of "four-winged", predatory dinosaurs.

dinosaurs.name: Chaoyangsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chaoyangsaurus ("Chaoyang lizard") is a genus of marginocephalian dinosaur from the Late Jurassic (about between 150.8 and 145.5 million years ago) of China.[1] Chaoyangsaurus belonged to the Ceratopsia (Greek for "horned faces").

dinosaurs.name: Charonosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Charonosaurus (/kəˌroʊnəˈsɔːrəs/ kə-ROH-nə-SOR-əs; meaning "Charon's lizard") is a genus of lambeosaurine hadrosaurid dinosaur similar to Parasaurolophus that lived in the latest Cretaceous Yuliangze Formation of what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Chasmosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chasmosaurus (/ˌkæzmoʊˈsɔːrəs/ KAZ-moh-SOR-əs) is a genus of ceratopsid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period in North America.

dinosaurs.name: Chebsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chebsaurus is a genus of sauropod dinosaur, specifically a eusauropod.

dinosaurs.name: Chenanisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chenanisaurus (meaning "Sidi Chennane lizard") is a genus of theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous in what is now Morocco.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Chenanisaurus barbaricus jaw by Nick Longrich

dinosaurs.name: Chialingosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chialingosaurus (meaning "Chialing Lizard") is a genus of herbivorous stegosaurian dinosaur similar to Kentrosaurus from the Upper Shaximiao Formation, Late Jurassic beds in Sichuan Province in China.

dinosaurs.name: Chienkosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chienkosaurus ("lizard of Jiange County") is a dubious genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Kuangyuan Series of China.

dinosaurs.name: Chilantaisaurus
