dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chilantaisaurus ("Jilantai Salt Lake lizard") is a genus of large theropod dinosaur, possibly a neovenatorid or a primitive coelurosaur, from the Late Cretaceous Ulansuhai Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Chilesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chilesaurus is an extinct genus of herbivorous dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Chindesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chindesaurus ( CHIN-diss-OR-əs) is an extinct genus of basal saurischian dinosaur from the Late Triassic (213-210 million years ago) of the southwestern United States.

dinosaurs.name: Chirostenotes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chirostenotes ( KY-roh-stin-OH-teez; named from Greek 'narrow-handed') is a genus of oviraptorosaurian dinosaur from the late Cretaceous (about 76.5–75 million years ago) of Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Chondrosteosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chondrosteosaurus (meaning "cartilage and bone lizard") was a sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Wessex Formation of England.

dinosaurs.name: Choyrodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Choyrodon is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Albian) Khuren Dukh Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Chuanjiesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chuanjiesaurus is a genus of sauropod dinosaurs from the middle Jurassic Period.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Chuanjiesaurus fossil in China Science and Technology Museum

dinosaurs.name: Chuanqilong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chuanqilong (meaning "legendary dragon") is a monospecific genus of basal ankylosaurid dinosaur from the Liaoning Province, China that lived during the Early Cretaceous (late Barremian to Aptian stage, 122.0 to 118.9 Ma) in what is now the Jiufotang Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Chubutisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chubutisaurus (meaning "Chubut lizard") is a genus of somphospondylan sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian) Cerro Barcino Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Chucarosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chucarosaurus (meaning "indomitable reptile") is an extinct genus of titanosaurian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (middle Cenomanian–lower Turonian) Huincul Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Chungkingosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Chungkingosaurus (meaning "Chongqing Lizard") is a genus of stegosaurian dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Upper Shaximiao Formation in what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Cionodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cionodon (meaning 'column tooth') is a dubious genus of hadrosaurid[3] dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Citipati
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Citipati ([ˈtʃiːt̪ɪpət̪i]; meaning "funeral pyre lord") is a genus of oviraptorid dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous period, about 75 million to 71 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Citipes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Citipes (/ˈsɪtipɛz/) is an extinct genus of caenagnathid theropod from the Late Cretaceous Dinosaur Park Formation in Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Claosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Claosaurus (/ˌkleɪəˈsɔːrəs/ KLAY-ə-SOR-əs; Greek κλάω, klao meaning 'broken' and σαῦρος, sauros meaning 'lizard'; "broken lizard", referring to the odd position of the fossils when discovered) is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous Period (Santonian-Campanian)[2] of what is now North America.

dinosaurs.name: Coahuilaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Coahuilaceratops (meaning "horned face from Coahuila") is a genus of chasmosaurine ceratopsian dinosaur that lived during the early Maastrichtian age of the Late Cretaceous epoch, about 71.5 to 70.5 million years ago in what is now northern Mexico.[1] It conta

dinosaurs.name: Coahuilasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Coahuilasaurus (meaning "Coahuila lizard") is an extinct genus of kritosaurin ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) Cerro del Pueblo Formation of Coahuila, Mexico.

dinosaurs.name: Coelophysis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Coelophysis (/sɛˈlɒfɪsɪs/ se-LOF-iss-iss traditionally; /ˌsɛloʊˈfaɪsɪs/ SEL-oh-FY-siss or /ˌsiːloʊˈfaɪsɪs/ SEE-loh-FY-siss[a]) is an extinct genus of coelophysid theropod dinosaurs that lived approximately 215 to 201.4 million years ago during the late Triassic period from the middle Norian to Rhaetian age in what is now the southwestern United States.

dinosaurs.name: Coeluroides
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Coeluroides ("hollow form") is an extinct genus of theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous Period in what is now India.[1] It is based solely on the holotype caudal vertebrae GSI K27/562, K27/574 and K27/595, discovered in a layer of the Lameta Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Colepiocephale
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Colepiocephale (meaning "knucklehead") is a genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaur from Late Cretaceous (middle Campanian stage) deposits of Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Coloradisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Coloradisaurus (meaning "Los Colorados lizard") is a genus of massospondylid sauropodomorph dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Compsognathus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Compsognathus (/kɒmpˈsɒɡnəθəs/;[1] Greek kompsos/κομψός; "elegant", "refined" or "dainty", and gnathos/γνάθος; "jaw")[2] is a genus of small, bipedal, carnivorous theropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Compsosuchus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Compsosuchus (meaning "elegant crocodile") is a dubious genus of abelisauroid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Lameta Formation of India.

dinosaurs.name: Concavenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Concavenator (meaning "Cuenca hunter") is a genus of carcharodontosaurian dinosaur that lived in Spain during the Early Cretaceous epoch, about 125 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Conchoraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Conchoraptor (meaning "conch plunderer") is a genus of oviraptorid dinosaurs that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous epoch, about 70 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Condorraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Condorraptor is an extinct genus of megalosauroid theropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Convolosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Convolosaurus (/ˌkɒnvələˈsɔːrəs/, meaning "flocking lizard" after the concentration of juvenile fossils found) is a genus of basal ornithopod dinosaur from the Twin Mountains Formation from Proctor Lake in Comanche County, Texas.

dinosaurs.name: Coronosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Coronosaurus is a genus of centrosaurine ceratopsian dinosaurs which lived in the Late Cretaceous, in the middle Campanian stage.

dinosaurs.name: Corythoraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Corythoraptor (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Corythosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Corythosaurus (/kəˌrɪθəˈsɔːrəs/;[1] lit.

dinosaurs.name: Craspedodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Craspedodon (meaning 'edge tooth') is an extinct genus of ornithischian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Lonzée Member of Belgium.

dinosaurs.name: Craterosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Craterosaurus (meaning krater reptile or bowl reptile) is a dubious genus of stegosaurian dinosaur that lived during the Early Cretaceous (possibly Aptian stage) of the Woburn Sands Formation of England.

dinosaurs.name: Crichtonsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Crichtonsaurus (meaning "Crichton's lizard") is a dubious genus of herbivorous ankylosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous in what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Cristatusaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cristatusaurus is a genus of theropod dinosaur that lived during the Early Cretaceous period of what is now Niger, 112 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Crittendenceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Crittendenceratops (meaning "horned face from the Fort Crittenden Formation") is a genus of horned centrosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur from the late Campanian Fort Crittenden Formation of Arizona.

dinosaurs.name: Cruxicheiros
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cruxicheiros (meaning "cross hand") is a genus of theropod dinosaur which lived in the Middle Jurassic of England.

dinosaurs.name: Cryolophosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cryolophosaurus ( or; KRY-ə-LOH-fə-SAWR-əs) is a genus of large theropod dinosaur known from only a single species, Cryolophosaurus ellioti, from the Early Jurassic of Antarctica.

dinosaurs.name: Cryptosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cryptosaurus (meaning "hidden lizard") is a dubious genus of dinosaur known from a partial femur from the Late Jurassic of England.

dinosaurs.name: Cumnoria
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Cumnoria is a genus of basal iguanodontian dinosaur that lived during the Late Jurassic period (Kimmeridgian age) in what is now Oxfordshire, United Kingdom.

dinosaurs.name: Dacentrurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dacentrurus (meaning "tail full of points") is an extinct genus of stegosaurian dinosaur from the Late Jurassic and perhaps Early Cretaceous (154–140 million years ago) of Europe.

dinosaurs.name: Daemonosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Daemonosaurus is an extinct genus of possible theropod dinosaur from the Late Triassic of New Mexico.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Daemonosaurus skull cast displayed at Ruth Hall Museum of Paleontology (Ghost Ranch)

dinosaurs.name: Dahalokely
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dahalokely is an extinct genus of carnivorous abelisauroid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Turonian) of Madagascar.

dinosaurs.name: Dakotadon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dakotadon (meaning "Dakota tooth") is a genus of iguanodont dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Valanginian to Barremian) Lakota Formation of South Dakota, USA, known from a partial skull.

dinosaurs.name: Dakotaraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dakotaraptor (meaning “thief from Dakota”) is a possible chimaeric genus of maniraptoriform theropod dinosaur that lived in western North America during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Daliansaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Daliansaurus (meaning "Dalian reptile") is a genus of small troodontid theropod dinosaur, measuring approximately 1 metre (3 ft 3 in) long, from the Early Cretaceous of China.

dinosaurs.name: Dandakosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Early Jurassic (Latest Pliensbachian-late Toarcian), 183-175 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Dandakosaurus (meaning "Dandakaranya lizard") is a dubious genus of theropod dinosaur from the Kota Formation, Andhra Pradesh, India.

dinosaurs.name: Daspletosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Daspletosaurus ( das-PLEET-ə-SOR-əs; meaning "frightful lizard") is a genus of tyrannosaurid dinosaur that lived in Laramidia between about 77 and 74.4 million years ago, during the Late Cretaceous Period.

dinosaurs.name: Datai
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Datai is an extinct genus of ankylosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Zhoutian Formation of Jiangxi Province, China.

dinosaurs.name: Datousaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Datousaurus (meaning "chieftain lizard" or "big-head lizard"; originally named using the Malay datu, after its Chinese nickname qiulong - literally "chieftain dragon" - but also a pun on its big head; da tou means "big head" in Chinese)[1] is a sauropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Lower Shaximiao Formation in Sichuan province, China.

dinosaurs.name: Daurlong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Daurlong (meaning "Daur dragon") is an extinct genus of dromaeosaurid dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Aptian) Longjiang Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Daxiatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Daxiatitan[a] is a genus of sauropod dinosaur known from the Lower Cretaceous of Gansu, China.

dinosaurs.name: Deinocheirus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Deinocheirus (/ˌdaɪnoʊˈkaɪrəs/ DY-no-KY-rəs) is a genus of large ornithomimosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous around 70 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Deinodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Deinodon (Greek for "terrible tooth") is a dubious genus of tyrannosaurid dinosaur containing a single species, Deinodon horridus, which is known only from a set of teeth found in the Late Cretaceous Judith River Formation of Montana and named by paleontologis

dinosaurs.name: Deinonychus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Deinonychus (/daɪˈnɒnɪkəs/[4] dy-NON-ih-kəs; from Ancient Greek δεινός (deinós) 'terrible' and ὄνυξ (ónux), genitive ὄνυχος (ónukhos) 'claw') is a genus of dromaeosaurid theropod dinosaur with one described species, Deinonychus antirrhopus.

dinosaurs.name: Deltadromeus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Deltadromeus (meaning "delta runner") is an extinct genus of controversial theropod dinosaur that lived in present-day Morocco during the mid-Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Denversaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Denversaurus (meaning "Denver lizard") is a genus of panoplosaurin nodosaurid dinosaur from the late Maastrichtian of Late Cretaceous Western North America.

dinosaurs.name: Diabloceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Diabloceratops (/daɪˌæbloʊˈsɛrətɒps/ dy-AB-loh-SERR-ə-tops) is an extinct genus of centrosaurine ceratopsian dinosaur that lived approximately 81.4-81 million years ago during the latter part of the Cretaceous Period in what is now Utah, in the United States.[1] Diabloceratops was a medium-sized, moderately built, ground-dwelling, quadrupedal herbivore, that could grow up to an estimated 4.5 metres (15 ft) in length and 1.3 metric tons (1.4 short tons) in body mass.

dinosaurs.name: Diamantinasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Diamantinasaurus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod from Australia that lived during the early Late Cretaceous, about 94 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Dicraeosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dicraeosaurus (Gr. δικραιος, dikraios "bifurcated, double-headed" + Gr. σαυρος, sauros "lizard") is a genus of diplodocoid sauropod dinosaur that lived in what is now Lindi Region, Tanzania during the late Jurassic period.

dinosaurs.name: Didanodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Didanodon (meaning "beautiful-sided tooth")[3] is a dubious genus of hadrosaurid from the Campanian Dinosaur Park Formation of Alberta.

dinosaurs.name: Dilophosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dilophosaurus (/daɪˌloʊfəˈsɔːrəs, -foʊ-/[1] dy-LOH-fə-SOR-əs, -⁠foh-) is a genus of theropod dinosaurs that lived in what is now North America during the Early Jurassic, about 186 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Diluvicursor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Diluvicursor ("flood runner") is a genus of small ornithischian from the Lower Albian (Early Cretaceous) of Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Dineobellator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dineobellator (meaning Diné warrior, pronounced /dɪˌneɪoʊbəˈleɪtər/) is a genus of dromaeosaurid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian age) Ojo Alamo Formation (Naashoibito Member) of New Mexico, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Dinheirosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dinheirosaurus is a genus of diplodocid sauropod dinosaur that is known from fossils uncovered in modern-day Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Diplodocus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Diplodocus (/dɪˈplɒdəkəs/,[3][4] /daɪˈplɒdəkəs/,[4] or /ˌdɪploʊˈdoʊkəs/[3]) is an extinct genus of diplodocid sauropod dinosaurs known from the Late Jurassic of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Diplotomodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Diplotomodon (meaning "double cutting tooth") is a dubious genus of theropod dinosaur, from New Jersey.

dinosaurs.name: Diuqin
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Diuqin (meaning "bird of prey") is an extinct genus of unenlagiine theropod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous (Santonian) Bajo de la Carpa Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Dongbeititan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dongbeititan is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Yixian Formation of Beipiao, Liaoning, China.

dinosaurs.name: Dongyangosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dongyangosaurus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Dornraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dornraptor (meaning "Dorset robber or thief") is an extinct genus of averostran theropod dinosaur from the Early Jurassic (Late Sinemurian) of Charmouth, Dorset, England.

dinosaurs.name: Draconyx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Draconyx (meaning "dragon claw") is a genus of ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic of what is now Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Dracoraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dracoraptor (meaning "dragon thief") is a genus of coelophysoid dinosaur that lived during the Hettangian stage of the Early Jurassic Period of what is now Wales dated at about 201 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Dracovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dracovenator  is a genus of neotheropod dinosaur that lived approximately 201 to 199 million years ago during the early part of the Jurassic period in what is now South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Dreadnoughtus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dreadnoughtus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur containing a single species, Dreadnoughtus schrani.

dinosaurs.name: Dromaeosauroides
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dromaeosauroides is a genus of dromaeosaurid theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of what is now Denmark and possibly also England.

dinosaurs.name: Dromaeosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dromaeosaurus (; lit.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Dromaeosaurus in Canadian Museum of Nature

dinosaurs.name: Dromiceiomimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dromiceiomimus is a genus of ornithomimid theropod from the Late Cretaceous (early Maastrichtian) of Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Dromicosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Triassic or Early Jurassic, 227.3-184.2 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Dromicosaurus is a dubious genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Late Triassic or Early Jurassic of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Drusilasaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Drusilasaura is an extinct genus of possible lognkosaurian titanosaur sauropod dinosaur which lived during the late Cretaceous (Cenomanian-Turonian stage) of Santa Cruz Province of southern Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Dryosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dryosaurus ( DRY-ə-SOR-əs, meaning 'tree lizard', Greek δρῦς (drys) meaning 'tree, oak' and σαυρος (sauros) meaning 'lizard' (the name reflects the forested habitat, not a vague oak-leaf shape of its cheek teeth as is sometimes assumed)) is a genus of ornithopod dinosaur that lived in the Late Jurassic period.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Beneski Museum of Natural History Dryosaurus altus

dinosaurs.name: Dryptosauroides
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dryptosauroides (meaning "similar in form to Dryptosaurus") is a dubious genus of possibly abelisauroid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian) Lameta Formation of India.

dinosaurs.name: Dryptosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dryptosaurus ( DRIP-toh-SOR-əs) is a genus of eutyrannosaurian theropod dinosaur that lived on the island continent of Appalachia approximately 67-66 million years ago during the end of the Maastrichtian age of the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Dubreuillosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dubreuillosaurus is a genus of carnivorous megalosaurid theropod dinosaur from the middle Jurassic Period.

dinosaurs.name: Duonychus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Duonychus (meaning "two claws") is an extinct genus of therizinosaurid theropod dinosaurs that lived during the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian to early Coniacian age) of what is now Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Duriatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Duriatitan is a genus of titanosauriform sauropod dinosaur that lived in the Late Jurassic in what is now England.

dinosaurs.name: Duriavenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Duriavenator is a genus of theropod dinosaur that lived in what is now England during the Middle Jurassic, about 168 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Dynamoterror
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dynamoterror (meaning "powerful terror") is a monospecific genus of tyrannosaurid dinosaur from New Mexico that lived during the Late Cretaceous (lower Campanian age, 78 Ma) in what is now the upper Allison Member of the Menefee Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Dyoplosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dyoplosaurus (meaning "double-armoured lizard") is a monospecific genus of ankylosaurid dinosaur from Alberta that lived during the Late Cretaceous (middle Campanian, ~76.5–75 Ma) in what is now the Dinosaur Park Formation.[1] Dyoplosaurus represents a close r

dinosaurs.name: Dysalotosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dysalotosaurus ("uncatchable lizard") is a genus of herbivorous iguanodontian dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Dyslocosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dyslocosaurus (meaning "hard-to-place lizard") a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Period of Wyoming, North America.

dinosaurs.name: Dystrophaeus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dystrophaeus is an extinct genus of sauropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Dzharaonyx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dzharaonyx (meaning "Dzharakuduk claw", named after the type locality) is a genus of alvarezsaurid theropod from the Late Cretaceous Bissekty Formation of Uzbekistan.

dinosaurs.name: Dzharatitanis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Dzharatitanis (meaning "Dzharakuduk titan") is an extinct genus of probable titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Turonian age) Bissekty Formation of Uzbekistan.

dinosaurs.name: Echinodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Echinodon is a genus of heterodontosaurid dinosaur that lived during the earliest Cretaceous of southern England and possibly western France in the Berriasian epoch.

dinosaurs.name: Edmontonia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Edmontonia is a genus of panoplosaurin nodosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period of what is now western North America.

dinosaurs.name: Edmontosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Edmontosaurus (/ɛdˌmɒntəˈsɔːrəs/ ed-MON-tə-SOR-əs) (meaning "lizard from Edmonton"), with the second species often colloquially and historically known as Anatosaurus or Anatotitan (meaning "duck lizard" and "giant duck"), is a genus of hadrosaurid (duck-billed) dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Efraasia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Efraasia (pronounced "E-FRAHS-ee-A") is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Einiosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Einiosaurus is a genus of herbivorous centrosaurine ceratopsian dinosaur from the Upper Cretaceous (Campanian stage) of northwestern Montana.

dinosaurs.name: Ekrixinatosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ekrixinatosaurus ('explosion-born reptile') is a genus of abelisaurid theropod which lived approximately 99 to 97 million years ago during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Elaltitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Elaltitan is an extinct genus of large lithostrotian titanosaur sauropod dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous (mid Cenomanian to Turonian stage) of Chubut Province, southern Argentina.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Elaltitan lilloi Skeletal Mk II Gunnar Bivens

dinosaurs.name: Elaphrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Elaphrosaurus (/ɛˌlɑːfroʊˈsɔːrəs/ el-AH-froh-SOR-əs) is a genus of ceratosaurian theropod dinosaur that lived approximately 155 to 145 million years ago during the Late Jurassic Period in what is now Tanzania in Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Elemgasem
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Elemgasem (after a Tehuelche god of the same name) is an extinct genus of brachyrostran abelisaurid from the Late Cretaceous Portezuelo Formation of Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Elmisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Elmisaurus (meaning "foot sole lizard") is an extinct genus of caenagnathid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Nemegt Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Elopteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Elopteryx is a genus of paravian theropod dinosaur based on fragmentary fossils found in Late Cretaceous rocks of Romania.

dinosaurs.name: Elrhazosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Elrhazosaurus (meaning "Elrhaz lizard") is a genus of basal iguanodontian dinosaur, known from isolated bones found in Early Cretaceous rocks of Niger.

dinosaurs.name: Embasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Embasaurus (meaning "Emba lizard") is a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Emiliasaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Emiliasaura (meaning "Emilia's lizard") is an extinct genus of iguanodontian ornithopod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous (Valanginian) Mulichinco Formation of Neuquén Province, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Enigmacursor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Enigmacursor (meaning "puzzle runner") is an extinct genus of neornithischian dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Morrison Formation of Colorado, United States.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Skeletal and life reconstruction of Enigmacursor

dinosaurs.name: Enigmosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Enigmosaurus (meaning "enigma lizard" or "enigmatic lizard") is a genus of therizinosauroid that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Eoabelisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eoabelisaurus  is a genus of abelisauroid theropod dinosaur from the Lower Jurassic Cañadón Asfalto Formation of the Cañadón Asfalto Basin in Argentina, South America.

dinosaurs.name: Eocarcharia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eocarcharia (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Eocursor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eocursor (meaning "dawn runner") is genus of basal ornithischian dinosaur that lived in what is now South Africa during the Early Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Eodromaeus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eodromaeus (meaning "dawn runner") is an extinct genus of probable basal theropod dinosaurs from the Late Triassic of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Eolambia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eolambia (meaning "dawn lambeosaurine") is a genus of herbivorous hadrosauroid dinosaur from the early Late Cretaceous of the United States.

dinosaurs.name: Eoneophron
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eoneophron (meaning "dawn Neophron") is an extinct genus of caenagnathid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Hell Creek Formation of South Dakota, US.

dinosaurs.name: Eoraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eoraptor  is a genus of small, lightly built, basal sauropodomorph dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Eosinopteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eosinopteryx is an extinct genus of theropod dinosaurs known to the Late Jurassic epoch of China.

dinosaurs.name: Eotrachodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eotrachodon (meaning "dawn Trachodon") is a genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur that lived in the Late Cretaceous of eastern North America.

dinosaurs.name: Eotriceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eotriceratops (meaning "dawn three-horned face") is a genus of herbivorous ceratopsian dinosaurs which lived in the area of North America during the late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Eotyrannus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eotyrannus (meaning "dawn tyrant") is a genus of tyrannosauroid theropod dinosaur hailing from the Early Cretaceous Wessex Formation beds, included in Wealden Group, located in the southwest coast of the Isle of Wight, United Kingdom.

dinosaurs.name: Epachthosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Epachthosaurus (meaning "heavy lizard") was a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur, belonging to Lithostrotia, from the Late Cretaceous of Central and Northern Patagonia in South America.

dinosaurs.name: Epichirostenotes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Epichirostenotes (meaning "above Chirostenotes", because it lived after the latter genus) is a genus of oviraptorosaurian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Epidexipteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Epidexipteryx is a genus of small maniraptoran dinosaurs, known from one fossil specimen in the collection of the Institute of Vertebrate Paleontology and Paleoanthropology in Beijing.

dinosaurs.name: Equijubus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Equijubus (Chinese: 马鬃龙; pinyin: Mǎzōng lóng; Mǎzōng meaning "horse mane" after the area Mǎzōng Mountain 马鬃山 in which it was found), is a genus of herbivorous hadrosauroid dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Albian stage) of northwestern China.

dinosaurs.name: Erectopus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Erectopus (meaning "upright foot") is an extinct genus of basal allosauroid theropod from the Early Cretaceous La Penthiève Beds Formation of France and also possibly the Cernavodă Formation of southern Romania.

dinosaurs.name: Erketu
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Erketu (meaning "Erketü tengri") is a genus of somphospondylan dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous, roughly between 96 million and 89 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Erliansaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Erliansaurus (meaning "Erlian lizard") is a genus of therizinosaurian theropod dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Cenomanian stage of the Late Cretaceous period in what is now Nei Mongol, Iren Dabasu Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Erlikosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Erlikosaurus (meaning "Erlik's lizard") is a genus of therizinosaurid that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Eucamerotus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eucamerotus (meaning "well-chambered", in reference to the hollows of the vertebrae) is an extinct genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Barremian) Wessex Formation (Wealden) of the Isle of Wight, England.

dinosaurs.name: Eucercosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eucercosaurus (meaning "good-tailed lizard"[3]) is the name given to a genus of ornithopod dinosaur from the Albian stage of the Early Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Eucnemesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eucnemesaurus (/juːkˌniːmɪˈsɔːrəs/; meaning "good tibia lizard", for its robust tibiae) is a basal sauropodomorph dinosaur genus usually considered to be a synonym of Euskelosaurus.

dinosaurs.name: Euhelopus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Euhelopus is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived between 143 and 133 million years ago during the Berriasian and Valanginian ages of the Early Cretaceous[1] in what is now Shandong Province in China.

dinosaurs.name: Euoplocephalus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Euoplocephalus (/juːˌɒploʊˈsɛfələs/ yoo-OP-loh-SEF-ə-ləs) is a genus of large herbivorous ankylosaurid dinosaurs, living during the Late Cretaceous of Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Euronychodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Euronychodon ("European claw tooth") is the name given to a genus of coelurosaur dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Europe and Asia.

dinosaurs.name: Europasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Europasaurus (meaning 'Europe lizard') is a basal macronarian sauropod, a form of quadrupedal herbivorous dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Europatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Early CretaceousLate Barremian-Early Aptian, 124-120 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Europatitan is an extinct genus of somphospondylan sauropod from the Early Cretaceous Castrillo de la Reina Formation of Iberia, known from a relatively complete specimen discovered in the early 2000s.

dinosaurs.name: Europelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Europelta (meaning "Europe's shield") is a monospecific genus of nodosaurid dinosaur from Spain that lived during the Early Cretaceous (early Albian stage, ~113.0 Ma) in what is now the lower Escucha Formation of the Teruel Province.[1] The type and only species, Europelta carbonensis, is known from two associated partial skeletons, and represents the most complete ankylosaur known from Europe.

dinosaurs.name: Euskelosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Euskelosaurus ("good leg lizard") is a sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Late Triassic of South Africa and Lesotho.

dinosaurs.name: Eustreptospondylus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Eustreptospondylus (/juːˌstrɛptoʊspɒnˈdaɪləs/ yoo-STREPT-o-spon-DY-ləs;[1]), from Ancient Greek εὖ (eû), meaning "well", στρεπτός (streptós), meaning "twisted", and σπόνδυλος (spóndulos), meaning "vertebra", is a genus of megalosaurid theropod dinosaur, from t

dinosaurs.name: Fabrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fabrosaurus (/ˌfæbrəˈsɔːrəs/[2] FAB-rə-SOR-əs) is a dubious extinct genus of ornithischian dinosaur that lived during the Early Jurassic during the Hettangian to Sinemurian stages (199 - 189 mya).

dinosaurs.name: Falcarius
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Falcarius (meaning "sickle cutter") is a genus of primitive therizinosaur dinosaur that lived during the Early Cretaceous period in what is now North America.

dinosaurs.name: Ferrisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ferrisaurus is a genus of leptoceratopsid ceratopsian dinosaur from the Sustut Basin (Tango Creek Formation) in British Columbia, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Fona
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fona (/ˈfoʊtnɑː/ foht-NAH; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Foraminacephale
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Foraminacephale (meaning "foramina head") is a genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaur from Late Cretaceous (Campanian stage) deposits of Canada.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Fosterovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fosterovenator (meaning "Foster's hunter") is a genus of ceratosaur dinosaur known from the Late Jurassic Morrison Formation of Wyoming.

dinosaurs.name: Fostoria
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fostoria (named after Robert Foster who discovered the type locality and bones; the specific name dhimbangunmal means "sheep yard" in the languages of the Yuwaalaraay, Yuwaalayaay, and Gamilaraay peoples of Australia) is a genus of iguanodontian ornithopod dinosaur from the Griman Creek Formation of New South Wales, Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Fruitadens
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fruitadens is a genus of heterodontosaurid dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Fukuiraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fukuiraptor ("thief of Fukui") is a genus of medium-sized megaraptoran theropod dinosaur of the Early Cretaceous epoch (either Barremian or Aptian) that lived in what is now Japan.[1] Fukuiraptor is known from the Kitadani Formation[2] and possibly also the Se

dinosaurs.name: Fukuisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fukuisaurus (meaning "Fukui (Fortunate) lizard") is a genus of herbivorous ornithopod dinosaur that lived during the Early Cretaceous in what is now Japan.

dinosaurs.name: Fukuititan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fukuititan (meaning "giant from Fukui prefecture") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived during the Early Cretaceous (either Barremian or Aptian age) in the Kitadani Formation in what is now Japan.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: フクイティタンの化石

dinosaurs.name: Fukuivenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fukuivenator ("hunter of Fukui Prefecture") is an extinct genus of maniraptoran theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Kitadani Formation of Japan.

dinosaurs.name: Fulgurotherium
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fulgurotherium (meaning "Lightning beast") is a dubious genus of ornithischian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian) Griman Creek Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Furcatoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Furcatoceratops (meaning "forked horned face") is a genus of centrosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Judith River Formation of Montana.

dinosaurs.name: Futalognkosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Futalognkosaurus ( FOO-tə-long-ko-SAW-rəs; meaning "giant chief lizard") is a genus of titanosaurian dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Fylax
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Fylax (meaning "keeper") is a genus of hadrosauroid ornithopod from the Late Cretaceous Figuerola Formation of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Galeamopus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Galeamopus is a genus of herbivorous diplodocid sauropod dinosaurs.

dinosaurs.name: Galleonosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Galleonosaurus (meaning "galleon lizard" as the upper jaw bone resembles an upturned galleon) is a genus of basal ornithopod dinosaur from the Wonthaggi Formation of the Gippsland region of Victoria, Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Gallimimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gallimimus ( GAL-im-EYE-məs) is a genus of theropod dinosaur that lived in what is now Mongolia during the Late Cretaceous period, about seventy million years ago (mya).

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Gallimimus bullatus.001 - Natural History Museum of London

dinosaurs.name: Gandititan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gandititan is an extinct genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Zhoutian Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Gannansaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gannansaurus (meaning "Gannan lizard") is an extinct genus of somphospondylan sauropod dinosaur known from the latest Cretaceous Nanxiong Formation of Ganzhou Basin, Jiangxi Province of southern China.

dinosaurs.name: Ganzhousaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ganzhousaurus (meaning "Ganzhou lizard") is an extinct genus of oviraptorid dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous Nanxiong Formation of Nankang County, Ganzhou City of Jiangxi Province, southern China.

dinosaurs.name: Gargoyleosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gargoyleosaurus (meaning "gargoyle lizard") is one of the earliest ankylosaurs known from reasonably complete fossil remains.

dinosaurs.name: Garudimimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Garudimimus (meaning "Garuda mimic") is a genus of ornithomimosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Garumbatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Garumbatitan (meaning "Garumba giant") is an extinct genus of somphospondylan sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Arcillas de Morella Formation of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Gasosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gasosaurus (/ɡˈæsəˈsɔːrəs/) is a genus of tetanuran theropod that lived approximately 171.6 to 161.2 million years ago during the middle of the Jurassic Period.

dinosaurs.name: Gasparinisaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gasparinisaura (meaning "Gasparini's lizard") is a genus of herbivorous ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Gastonia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gastonia is a genus of herbivorous ankylosaurian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of North America, around 139 to 125.77 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Geminiraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Geminiraptor (meaning "twin seizer") is a genus of troodontid theropod dinosaur that lived in North America during the Early Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Genyodectes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Genyodectes ("jaw bite", from the Greek words genys ("jaw") and dektes ("bite")) is a genus of ceratosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Lower Cretaceous (Aptian) of South America.

dinosaurs.name: Geranosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Geranosaurus (meaning "crane reptile") is a genus of heterodontosaurid ornithischian dinosaur from the Early Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Gideonmantellia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gideonmantellia is an extinct genus of basal ornithopod dinosaur known from the Early Cretaceous (Barremian stage) Camarillas Formation of Galve, Province of Teruel, Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Giganotosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Giganotosaurus (/ˌɡɪɡəˌnoʊtəˈsɔːrəs/ GIG-ə-NOH-tə-SOR-əs[2]) is a genus of large theropod dinosaur that lived in what is now Argentina, during the early Cenomanian age of the Late Cretaceous period, approximately 99.6 to 95 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Gigantoraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gigantoraptor (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Gigantspinosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gigantspinosaurus (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Gilmoreosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gilmoreosaurus (meaning "Charles Whitney Gilmore's lizard") is a genus of hadrosauromorphan dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Asia.

dinosaurs.name: Giraffatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Giraffatitan (name meaning "titanic giraffe") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived during the late Jurassic Period (Kimmeridgian–Tithonian stages) in what is now Lindi Region, Tanzania.

dinosaurs.name: Glyptodontopelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Glyptodontopelta (meaning "Glyptodon shield") is a monospecific genus of nodosaurid dinosaur from New Mexico that lived during the Late Cretaceous (lower to upper Maastrichtian, 69 to 66 Ma) in what is now the Naashoibito member of the Ojo Alamo Formation.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Gnathovorax
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gnathovorax is a genus of herrerasaurid saurischian dinosaur from the Santa Maria Formation in Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Gobihadros
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gobihadros is a genus of basal hadrosauroid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period in what is now the Bayanshiree Formation (dated to the Cenomanian-Santonian) of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Gobiraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gobiraptor is a genus of oviraptorid maniraptoran dinosaur from the Maastrichtian-aged Nemegt Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Gobisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gobisaurus is an extinct genus of herbivorous basal ankylosaurid ankylosaur from the Lower Cretaceous of China (Nei Mongol Zizhiqu).

dinosaurs.name: Gobivenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gobivenator is an extinct genus of troodontid theropod dinosaur known from the late Campanian Djadokhta Formation of central Gobi Desert, Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Gojirasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Triassic, Revueltian (mid-late Norian), 212 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Gojirasaurus is a genus of coelophysoid theropod dinosaur named after the giant monster movie character Godzilla.

dinosaurs.name: Gondwanatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gondwanatitan (meaning "giant from Gondwana") was a titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Gongpoquansaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gongpoquansaurus (meaning "Gongpoquan reptile") is an extinct genus of basal hadrosauroid dinosaur that was not formally named until 2014, while the name was a nomen nudum for many years previously.

dinosaurs.name: Gonkoken
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gonkoken (meaning "similar to a wild duck or swan") is an extinct genus of hadrosauroid ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Dorotea Formation of Chilean Patagonia.

dinosaurs.name: Gorgosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gorgosaurus ( GOR-gə-SOR-əs; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Goyocephale
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Goyocephale is an extinct genus of pachycephalosaurian ornithischian that lived in Mongolia during the Late Cretaceous about 76 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Graciliraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Graciliraptor (meaning "graceful thief") is a genus of microraptorian dromaeosaurid dinosaur from the early Cretaceous Period.

dinosaurs.name: Gravitholus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gravitholus (meaning 'heavy dome') was a genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaur from the late Cretaceous period (Campanian stage, around 75 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Gresslyosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gresslyosaurus (meaning "Amanz Gressly's lizard") is a genus of plateosaurian sauropodomorph dinosaur that lived during the Late Triassic period, around 214 to 204 million years ago, in France, Germany, Norway, Greenland and Switzerland.

dinosaurs.name: Gryphoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gryphoceratops is an extinct genus of leptoceratopsid ceratopsian dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous of Alberta, southern Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Gryponyx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gryponyx (meaning "hooked-claw") is an extinct genus of massopod sauropodomorph known from southern Free State, central South Africa.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Annals of the South African Museum. Annale van die Suid-Afrikaanse Museum (18234532989)

dinosaurs.name: Gryposaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gryposaurus (meaning "hooked-nosed (Greek grypos) lizard"; sometimes incorrectly translated as "griffin (Latin gryphus) lizard") was a genus of duckbilled dinosaur that lived about 80 to 75 million years ago, in the Late Cretaceous (late Santonian to late Campanian stages) of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Guaibasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Guaibasaurus is an extinct genus of basal saurischian dinosaur known from the Late Triassic Caturrita Formation of Rio Grande do Sul, southern Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Guanlong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Guanlong (Chinese: 冠龍; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Guemesia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Guemesia (named after Martín Miguel de Güemes, whose death bicentenary was in 2021) is a genus of abelisaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Los Blanquitos Formation of Salta Province, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Gyposaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Gyposaurus (meaning "vulture lizard", referring to the outdated hypothesis that prosauropods were carnivores) is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur from the early Jurassic of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Hadrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hadrosaurus (/ˌhædrəˈsɔːrəs/; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Haestasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Haestasaurus is a genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur, belonging to the Macronaria, that during the Early Cretaceous lived in the area of present-day England.

dinosaurs.name: Hagryphus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hagryphus (meaning "Ha's griffin") is a monospecific genus of caenagnathid dinosaur from southern Utah that lived during the Late Cretaceous (upper Campanian stage, 75.95 Ma) in what is now the Kaiparowits Formation of the Grand Staircase–Escalante National Monument.[1] The type and only species, Hagryphus giganteus, is known only from an incomplete but articulated left manus and the distal portion of the left radius.[1] It was named in 2005 by Lindsay E.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Hagryphus giganteus - Natural History Museum of Utah - DSC07248

dinosaurs.name: Halszkaraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Halszkaraptor (/ˈhɑːlʃkəræptər/; meaning "Halszka's seizer") is a genus of waterfowl-like dromaeosaurid dinosaurs from Mongolia that lived during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Hamititan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hamititan (meaning "Hami giant") is a genus of titanosaur sauropod dinosaur from the Shengjinkou Formation of Xinjiang, China.

dinosaurs.name: Hanssuesia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: †Hanssuesia sternbergi (Brown & Schlaikjer, 1943)

dinosaurs.name: Haplocanthosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Haplocanthosaurus (meaning "simple spined lizard") is a genus of diplodocoid sauropod dinosaur.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Haplocanthosaurus in Cleveland Museum of Natural History

dinosaurs.name: Haplocheirus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Haplocheirus (/ˌhæplʊuˈkəirəs/, from Ancient Greek ἁπλός (haplós), meaning "simple", and χείρ (kheír), meaning "hand") is an extinct genus of theropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Shishugou Formation of Xinjiang in China.

dinosaurs.name: Harenadraco
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Harenadraco (meaning "sand dragon") is an extinct genus of troodontid theropod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous Baruungoyot Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Harpymimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Harpymimus is a genus of basal ornithomimosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Period of what is now Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Helioceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Helioceratops is a genus of herbivorous neoceratopsian dinosaur from the Middle Cretaceous of China.

dinosaurs.name: Heptasteornis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Heptasteornis is a dubious genus of alvarezsaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Herrerasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Herrerasaurus is likely a genus of saurischian dinosaur from the Late Triassic period.

dinosaurs.name: Hesperonychus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hesperonychus (meaning "western claw") is a genus of small dromaeosaurid dinosaur.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Hesperonychus elizabethae pelvis by Nick Longrich

dinosaurs.name: Hesperonyx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hesperonyx (meaning "western claw") is an extinct genus of dryomorphan ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Lourinhã Formation of Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Hesperornithoides
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hesperornithoides (meaning "western bird form"; nicknamed "Lori") is a genus of troodontid theropod dinosaur that lived in North America during the Late Jurassic period.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Hesperosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hesperosaurus (from Ancient Greek ἕσπερος (hésperos), meaning "western", and σαῦρος (saûros), meaning "lizard") is a herbivorous stegosaurian dinosaur from the Kimmeridgian age of the Jurassic period, approximately 156 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Heterodontosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Heterodontosaurus is a genus of heterodontosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Early Jurassic, 200–190 million years ago.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Heterodontosaurus tucki cast - University of California Museum of Paleontology - Berkeley, CA - DSC04696

dinosaurs.name: Hexinlusaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hexinlusaurus is a genus of basal ornithischian dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic (Bajocian stage) Lower Shaximiao Formation of what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Heyuannia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Heyuannia ("from Heyuan") is a genus of oviraptorid dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous epoch, in what is now China and Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Hierosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hierosaurus (meaning "sacred lizard") is an extinct genus of nodosaurid ankylosaur which lived during the Late Cretaceous 87 to 82 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Hippodraco
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hippodraco is a genus of iguanodontian ornithopod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Cedar Mountain Formation of Utah, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Histriasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Histriasaurus (HIS-tree-ah-SAWR-us) (meaning "Istria lizard") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Hauterivian to Barremian stages, around 135-125 million years ago) of Croatia.

dinosaurs.name: Homalocephale
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Homalocephale (from Greek ὁμαλός, homalos, "even", and κεφαλή, kephalē, "head") is a genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period of what is now the Nemegt Formation, Mongolia, about 70 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Hoplitosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hoplitosaurus (meaning "Hoplite lizard") was a genus of armored dinosaur related to Polacanthus.

dinosaurs.name: Hortalotarsus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hortalotarsus is a dubious genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Early Jurassic of southern Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Huabeisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huabeisaurus (, meaning "North China lizard") was a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian to Maastrichtian stages, around 99.7–70.6 million years ago) of what is present-day northern China.

dinosaurs.name: Huadanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huadanosaurus is an extinct genus of compsognathid-like theropod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous Yixian Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Hualianceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hualianceratops is a genus of herbivorous ceratopsian dinosaur that lived about 160 million years ago in the Late Jurassic epoch in what is now western China.

dinosaurs.name: Huallasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huallasaurus (meaning "duck lizard") is an extinct genus of saurolophine hadrosaur from the Late Cretaceous Los Alamitos Formation of Patagonia in Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Huanansaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huanansaurus is an extinct genus of oviraptorid dinosaur that lived approximately 72 million years ago, between the Campanian and Maastrichtian, during the latter part of the Cretaceous period in what is now China, in the Nanxiong Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Huanghetitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huanghetitan (meaning "Yellow River titan"), is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the early Cretaceous Period.

dinosaurs.name: Huangshanlong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huangshanlong (meaning "Huangshan dragon") is a genus of mamenchisaurid dinosaurs native to the Anhui province of China.

dinosaurs.name: Huaxiagnathus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huaxiagnathus is an extinct genus of coelurosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Lower Cretaceous of China.

dinosaurs.name: Huayangosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huayangosaurus is a genus of stegosaurian dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic of China.

dinosaurs.name: Huayracursor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huayracursor (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Hudiesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hudiesaurus (meaning "butterfly lizard") is an extinct genus of mamenchisaurid sauropod dinosaur that lived in present-day China during the Late Jurassic period.

dinosaurs.name: Huehuecanauhtlus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huehuecanauhtlus is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous (Santonian stage) of Michoacán, western Mexico.

dinosaurs.name: Huinculsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Huinculsaurus is an extinct genus of noasaurid dinosaurs known from the Late Cretaceous Huincul Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Hulsanpes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hulsanpes (meaning "Khulsan foot") is a genus of halszkaraptorine theropod dinosaurs that lived during the Late Cretaceous in what is now the Barun Goyot Formation of Mongolia, about 75-72 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Hungarosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hungarosaurus (meaning 'Hungarian lizard' from the Latin Hungaria, 'Hungary', and Greek σαυρος/sauros, 'lizard') is an extinct genus of nodosaurid ankylosaurian dinosaur from the Upper Cretaceous (Santonian) Csehbánya Formation of the Bakony Mountains, western Hungary.

dinosaurs.name: Hylaeosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hylaeosaurus (/haɪˌliːoʊˈsɔːrəs/ hy-LEE-o-SOR-əs) is a herbivorous ankylosaurian dinosaur that lived about 136 million years ago, in the late Valanginian stage of the early Cretaceous period of England.

dinosaurs.name: Hypacrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hypacrosaurus (meaning "near the highest lizard" [from Ancient Greek ὑπο- hypo- "less" and ἄκρος akros "high"], because it was almost but not quite as large as Tyrannosaurus)[2][3] is an extinct genus of duckbill dinosaur similar in appearance to Corythosaurus.

dinosaurs.name: Hypnovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hypnovenator (meaning "sleep hunter") is an extinct genus of troodontid theropod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous (Albian) Ohyamashimo Formation of Japan.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Hypnovenator matsubaraetoheorum fossils and reconstructed skeleton

dinosaurs.name: Hypselosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hypselosaurus (meaning 'highest lizard', from Greek ὑψηλός meaning 'high' or 'lofty' and σαυρος meaning 'lizard') is a dubious genus of titanosaurian sauropod that lived in southern France during the Late Cretaceous, approximately 70 million years ago in the early Maastrichtian.

dinosaurs.name: Hypselospinus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hypselospinus is a genus of iguanodontian dinosaur which was first described as a species of Iguanodon (I. fittoni) by Richard Lydekker in 1889, the specific name honouring William Henry Fitton.

dinosaurs.name: Hypsibema
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hypsibema is an extinct genus of very large basal hadrosauroid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) of eastern North America.

dinosaurs.name: Hypsilophodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hypsilophodon (/ˌhɪpsɪˈlɒfoʊdɒn/; meaning "high-crested tooth") is a neornithischian dinosaur genus from the Early Cretaceous period of England.

dinosaurs.name: Hypsirhophus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Hypsirhophus (meaning "high roof"; often misspelled "Hypsirophus") is a genus of stegosaurian dinosaurs.

dinosaurs.name: Iberospinus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Iberospinus (meaning "Iberian spine") is an extinct genus of spinosaurid dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Barremian) Papo Seco Formation of Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Ichthyovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ichthyovenator is a genus of spinosaurid dinosaur that lived in what is now Laos, sometime between 120 and 113 million years ago, during the Aptian stage of the Early Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Igai
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Igai is a genus of titanosaur from the Late Cretaceous Quseir Formation of Kharga Oasis, Egypt.

dinosaurs.name: Iguanacolossus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Iguanacolossus (meaning "iguana colossus" or "colossal iguana") is a genus of iguanodontian ornithopod dinosaur that lived in North America during the Early Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Iguanodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Iguanodon (/ɪˈɡwɑːnədɒn/ i-GWAH-nə-don; meaning 'iguana-tooth'), named in 1825, is a genus of iguanodontian dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Iliosuchus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Iliosuchus (meaning "ilium crocodile") is a genus of theropod dinosaur known from Bathonian-aged (168.3 – 166.1 mya) rocks of England.

dinosaurs.name: Imperobator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Imperobator ("powerful warrior") is a genus of probable unenlagiid paravian theropod dinosaurs, that lived during the Maastrichtian age of the Late Cretaceous in what is now James Ross Island in Antarctica.

dinosaurs.name: Inawentu
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Inawentu (meaning "imitator") is an extinct genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Bajo de la Carpa Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Incisivosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Incisivosaurus ("incisor lizard") is a genus of small, probably herbivorous theropod dinosaurs from the early Cretaceous Period of what is now the People's Republic of China.

dinosaurs.name: Indosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Indosaurus (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Indosuchus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Indosuchus (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Invictarx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Invictarx (meaning "unconquerable fortress") is a monospecific genus of nodosaurid dinosaur from New Mexico that lived during the Late Cretaceous (lower Campanian, 78.5 Ma) in what is now the upper Allison Member of the Menefee Formation.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Invictarx zephyri holotype PeerJ e5435 fig 4

dinosaurs.name: Irisosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Irisosaurus (meaning "iridescent lizard" after the iridescent clouds of Yunnan Province, China) is an extinct genus of sauropodiform sauropodomorph dinosaur,[1] from the Fengjiahe Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Irritator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Irritator is a genus of spinosaurid dinosaur that lived in what is now Brazil during the Albian stage of the Early Cretaceous Period, about 113 to 110 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Isaberrysaura
