dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Isaberrysaura is an extinct genus of stegosaurian ornithischian dinosaurs from the Middle Jurassic Los Molles Formation of Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Isanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Triassic or Late Jurassic, 214-145 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Isanosaurus ("North-eastern Thailand lizard") is an extinct genus of sauropod dinosaur from Thailand.

dinosaurs.name: Ischioceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ischioceratops (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Isisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Isisaurus (named after the Indian Statistical Institute) is a genus of titanosaurian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Lameta Formation of India and Pab Formation of Pakistan.

dinosaurs.name: Issi
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Issi (meaning "cold" in Greenlandic) is a plateosaurid dinosaur from the Late Triassic Fleming Fjord Formation of Greenland.

dinosaurs.name: Itemirus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Itemirus is a genus of dromaeosaurid theropod dinosaur from the Turonian aged Bissekty Formation of the Late Cretaceous period of Uzbekistan.

dinosaurs.name: Iuticosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Iuticosaurus (meaning "Jute lizard") is a genus of titanosaur sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of the Isle of Wight.

dinosaurs.name: Jaculinykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jaculinykus (meaning "Jaculus claw") is an extinct genus of alvarezsaurid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Baruungoyot Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Jainosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jainosaurus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur of India and wider Asia, which lived in the Maastrichtian (approximately 68 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Jakapil
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Early Cretaceous-Late Cretaceous, Aptian-Cenomanian, 119-94 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Jakapil (meaning "shield bearer" in Puelchean) is a genus of basal thyreophoran dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian-aged) Candeleros Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Janenschia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Janenschia (named after Werner Janensch) is a genus of large herbivorous sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic (around 155 to 145 million years ago) Tendaguru Formation of Lindi Region, Tanzania.

dinosaurs.name: Jaxartosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jaxartosaurus (meaning "Jaxartes lizard" after the early name of the Syr Darya) is a genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur similar to Corythosaurus which lived during the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Jeholosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jeholosaurus is a genus of small neornithischian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous-aged Yixian Formation of what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Jeyawati
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jeyawati (meaning "grinding mouth" in the Zuni language) is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur which lived during the Turonian stage of the Late Cretaceous.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Skeletons of Lanzhousaurus magnidens and Jintasaurus meniscus

dinosaurs.name: Jianchangosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jianchangosaurus ("lizard from Jianchang County") is a genus of therizinosaurian dinosaur that lived approximately 126 million years ago during the early part of the Cretaceous Period from the Yixian Formation in what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Jiangjunosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jiangjunosaurus is an extinct genus of herbivorous stegosaurian dinosaurs from the Oxfordian-age (Upper Jurassic) Shishugou Formation of the Junggar Basin, Xinjiang, China.

dinosaurs.name: Jiangshanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jiangshanosaurus is a genus of herbivorous titanosauriform sauropod dinosaur that lived in China approximately 92-88 million years ago,[1] during the Turonian and Coniacian stages of the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Jiangxisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jiangxisaurus is an extinct genus of oviraptorid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Nanxiong Formation of southern China.

dinosaurs.name: Jiangxititan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jiangxititan is an extinct genus of somphospondylan titanosauriform dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Nanxiong Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Jianianhualong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jianianhualong (meaning "Jianianhua dragon") is a genus of troodontid theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of China.

dinosaurs.name: Jinfengopteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jinfengopteryx (from Jinfeng, 'golden phoenix', the queen of birds in Chinese folklore, and Ancient Greek: πτερυξ pteryx, meaning 'feather') is a genus of maniraptoran dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Jingiella
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jingiella is an extinct genus of mamenchisaurid sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Dongxing Formation of Guangxi, China.

dinosaurs.name: Jingshanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jingshanosaurus (meaning "Jingshan lizard") is a genus of sauropodomorph dinosaurs from the early Jurassic period (201.4 to 196.5 million years ago, Hettangian age) of China.

dinosaurs.name: Jintasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jintasaurus (Chinese: 金塔龙; pinyin: Jīntǎ lóng; meaning "Jinta County dragon") is a genus of hadrosauriform dinosaur that lived during the Early Cretaceous of what is now Jiuquan, Gansu, northwestern China.

dinosaurs.name: Jinyunpelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jinyunpelta ("Jinyun shield") is a genus of herbivorous ankylosaurine thyreophoran dinosaur from the Cretaceous Liangtoutang Formation of Jinyun County, Zhejiang, China; it has one species, the type species J. sinensis.

dinosaurs.name: Jinzhousaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jinzhousaurus is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur of the Early Cretaceous of China.

dinosaurs.name: Joaquinraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Joaquinraptor (wah-KEEN-rap-tor; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Jobaria
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jobaria is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived in what is now Niger during the middle Jurassic Period, between 164 and 161 million years ago.[1] Jobaria is currently the only known valid sauropod from the Tiouraren, where it was discovered in 1997.

dinosaurs.name: Jubbulpuria
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Jubbulpuria ("Jubbulpore one") is the name given to a dubious genus of small dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Lameta Formation of India.

dinosaurs.name: Judiceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Judiceratops (/ˌdʒuːdiːˈsɛrətɒps/ JOO-dee-SERR-ə-tops; meaning "Judith River horned face") is an extinct horned dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Juratyrant
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Juratyrant (meaning "Jurassic tyrant") is a genus of tyrannosauroid dinosaur from the late Jurassic period (early Tithonian age) of England.

dinosaurs.name: Juravenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Juravenator is a genus of tetanuran theropod dinosaur which lived about 152 or 151 million years ago in modern day Franconian Jura of Germany (Torleite Formation[1]) during the Late Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Kaatedocus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kaatedocus is a genus of flagellicaudatan sauropod known from the middle Late Jurassic (Kimmeridgian stage) of northern Wyoming, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Kaijiangosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kaijiangosaurus (meaning "Kaijiang lizard") is a genus of carnivorous tetanuran theropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic of China.

dinosaurs.name: Kakuru
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kakuru is a dubious genus of theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Kamuysaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kamuysaurus is a genus of herbivorous edmontosaurin saurolophine hadrosaurid dinosaur from Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian) marine deposits of the Yezo Group (Hakobuchi Formation) in the Hobetsu area near the town of Mukawa, Hokkaido in Japan.[1][2]

dinosaurs.name: Kangnasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kangnasaurus (meaning "Farm Kangnas lizard") is a genus of elasmarian ornithopod dinosaur found in Late Cretaceous rocks of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Kansaignathus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kansaignathus (/ˌkɛnsɑːiˈnɛiθəs/) is an extinct genus of dromaeosaurid theropod from the Late Cretaceous Yalovach Formation (or Ialovachsk Formation) of Tajikistan.

dinosaurs.name: Karongasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Karongasaurus (meaning Karonga lizard) is a genus of titanosaur sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Katepensaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Katepensaurus ("hole lizard") is an extinct genus of rebbachisaurid sauropod dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous of south-central Chubut Province of central Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Kazaklambia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kazaklambia is an extinct genus of herbivorous lambeosaurine dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous Dabrazinskaya Svita (Santonian stage) of southern Kazakhstan.

dinosaurs.name: Kelmayisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kelmayisaurus (meaning "Karamay lizard") is an extinct genus of carcharodontosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Kelumapusaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kelumapusaura (meaning "red earth lizard") is a genus of saurolophine hadrosaur from the Late Cretaceous Allen Formation in what is now Patagonia in Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Kentrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kentrosaurus ( KEN-troh-SOR-əs; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Kerberosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kerberosaurus (meaning "Kerberos lizard") is a genus of saurolophine duckbill dinosaur from the late Maastrichtian-aged (Upper Cretaceous) Tsagayan Formation of Blagoveshchensk, Amur Region, Russia (dated to 66 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Khaan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Khaan (; from Mongol [χaːŋ] 'lord') is an extinct genus of oviraptorid dinosaur known from remains found in the Djadochta Formation of Mongolia, dating to the Late Cretaceous (Campanian, 75-71 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Kholumolumo
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kholumolumo (referring to a type of dragon the local Basuto associate with dinosaurs), formerly "Kholumolumosaurus" or "Thotobolosaurus", is an extinct genus of massopodan sauropodomorph dinosaur, which was closely related to Sarahsaurus, from the lower Elliot Formation of Maphutseng, Lesotho.

dinosaurs.name: Kileskus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kileskus (meaning "lizard" in the Khakas language) is a genus of tyrannosauroid dinosaur known from partial remains found in Middle Jurassic (Bathonian stage) Itat Formation of Sharypovsky District, Krasnoyarsk Krai (Russia).

dinosaurs.name: Kinnareemimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kinnareemimus (, meaning "Kinnaree mimic", after a figure from Thai folklore) is an extinct genus of ornithomimosaurian theropod dinosaur that was discovered in the Early Cretaceous Sao Khua Formation in what is now Thailand.

dinosaurs.name: Kiyacursor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kiyacursor (meaning "Kiya River runner") is an extinct genus of noasaurid theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Ilek Formation of Russia.

dinosaurs.name: Klamelisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Klamelisaurus (meaning "Kelameili Mountains lizard") is a genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Shishugou Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Koleken
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Koleken (meaning "coming from clay and water") is a genus of carnotaurin abelisaurid from the Maastrichtian La Colonia Formation in the Chubut Province of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Koparion
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Koparion is a genus of small coelurosaurian theropod dinosaur, from the late Jurassic Period (Kimmeridgian stage), of Utah.

dinosaurs.name: Koreaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Koreaceratops (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Koreanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Koreanosaurus (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Koshisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Koshisaurus is a monospecific genus of basal hadrosauroid from the Kitadani Formation in Japan.

dinosaurs.name: Kosmoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kosmoceratops (/ˌkɒzməˈsɛrətɒps/[1]) is a genus of ceratopsid dinosaur that lived in North America about 76–75.9 million years ago during the Late Cretaceous period.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: コスモケラトプス・リカルゾニ頭骨

dinosaurs.name: Kotasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kotasaurus (/ˌkoʊtəˈsɔːrəs/ KOH-tə-SOR-əs; meaning "Kota Formation lizard") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Jurassic period (Sinemurian–Pliensbachian[1]).

dinosaurs.name: Koutalisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Koutalisaurus (meaning "spoon lizard", in reference to the shape of the dentary) is a potentially dubious genus of lambeosaurine hadrosaurid dinosaur based on a mostly complete dentary recovered from the Maastrichtian-aged (Late Cretaceous) Tremp Formation nea

dinosaurs.name: Kritosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kritosaurus is an incompletely known genus of hadrosaurid (duck-billed) dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Kryptops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kryptops (meaning "covered face") is an extinct genus of abelisaurid theropod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous of Niger.

dinosaurs.name: Kulceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kulceratops is a genus of ceratopsian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Kulindadromeus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kulindadromeus is a genus of basal neornithischian dinosaur that lived during the Middle Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Kunbarrasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kunbarrasaurus (meaning "shield lizard") is an extinct genus of small ankylosaurian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Kundurosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Kundurosaurus is an extinct genus of saurolophine hadrosaurid dinosaur known from the latest Cretaceous (probably late Maastrichtian stage) of Amur Region, Far Eastern Russia.

dinosaurs.name: Labocania
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Labocania is an extinct genus of theropod dinosaur of disputed affinities, known from the Late Cretaceous of Mexico.

dinosaurs.name: Laevisuchus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Laevisuchus (/ˌlɛvɪˈsjuːkəs/, "light crocodile") is a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Laiyangosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Laiyangosaurus ("Laiyang lizard") is a genus of saurolophine hadrosaurid from the Late Cretaceous Jingangkou Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Lajasvenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lajasvenator (meaning "Las Lajas hunter" after the city of Las Lajas in Neuquén, Argentina) is a genus of carcharodontosaurid dinosaur from the Mulichinco Formation from Neuquén Province in Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Lambeosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lambeosaurus (/ˌlæmbiəˈsɔːrəs/ LAM-bee-ə-SOR-əs[7]) is a genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period of western North America.

dinosaurs.name: Lamplughsaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lamplughsaura is a genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Sinemurian-aged (Early Jurassic) Dharmaram Formation of India, dating from between 196 and 190 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Lanzhousaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lanzhousaurus (meaning "Lanzhou lizard") is a genus of ornithopod dinosaur that lived in the Gansu region of what is now China during the Early Cretaceous (Barremian).

dinosaurs.name: Laosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Laosaurus (meaning "stone or fossil lizard") is a genus of neornithischian dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Laplatasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Laplatasaurus (meaning "La Plata lizard", named for La Plata, Argentina) is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous in South America, with the holotype and only known specimen found in the Anacleto Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Lapparentosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lapparentosaurus is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Laquintasaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Laquintasaura is a genus of Venezuelan ornithischian dinosaur containing a single species, Laquintasaura venezuelae.

dinosaurs.name: Latenivenatrix
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Latenivenatrix, meaning "hiding huntress", is a genus of large troodontid known from a single species, L. mcmasterae.

dinosaurs.name: Latirhinus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Latirhinus (meaning "broad nose" from the Latin latus (broad) and Greek ῥίς, rhis (nose)) is an extinct genus of lambeosaurine hadrosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Mexico.

dinosaurs.name: Lavocatisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lavocatisaurus (meaning "René Lavocat's lizard") is a genus of sauropod in the family Rebbachisauridae from the Early Cretaceous (Aptian to Albian) Rayoso Formation of the Neuquén Basin, northern Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Leaellynasaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Leaellynasaura (meaning "Leaellyn's lizard") is a genus of small herbivorous ornithischian dinosaurs from the late Aptian to early Albian stage of the Early Cretaceous, around 118-110 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Ledumahadi
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ledumahadi (meaning "giant thunderclap" in Sesotho language) is a genus of lessemsaurid sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Early Jurassic Elliot Formation in Free State Province, South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Leinkupal
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Leinkupal is a genus of diplodocine sauropod known from the Early Cretaceous (Late Berriasian to Early Valanginian stage) of the Bajada Colorada Formation, southeastern Neuquén Basin in the Neuquén Province of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Leonerasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Leonerasaurus is a basal genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Leptoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Leptoceratops (meaning 'small horn face') is a genus of ceratopsian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Leptorhynchos
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Leptorhynchos  is an extinct genus of caenagnathid theropod from the Late Cretaceous of what is now the US state of Texas, although it has been suggested to also exist in Alberta and South Dakota.

dinosaurs.name: Leshansaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Leshansaurus is a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Shaximiao Formation of what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Lesothosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lesothosaurus is a monospecific genus of ornithischian dinosaur that lived during the Early Jurassic in what is now South Africa and Lesotho.

dinosaurs.name: Lessemsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lessemsaurus is an extinct genus of sauropodiform dinosaur that lived in the Norian-aged (Upper Triassic) Los Colorados Formation of what is now Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Lexovisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lexovisaurus is a genus of stegosaurian dinosaur known from limb bones and armor fragments from Middle to Late Jurassic-aged strata of Europe.

dinosaurs.name: Leyesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Leyesaurus is an extinct genus of massospondylid sauropodomorph dinosaur known from the San Juan Province, northwestern Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Liaoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Liaoceratops, meaning "Liaoning horned face", is a ceratopsian dinosaur believed to be an early relative of the horned ceratopsids.

dinosaurs.name: Liaoningosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Liaoningosaurus (meaning "Liaoning lizard") is an unusual genus of basal ankylosaurid dinosaur from the Liaoning Province, China that lived during the Early Cretaceous (late Barremian to early Aptian stages, ~125.4 to 118.9 Ma) in what is now the Yixian and Jiufotang Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Liaoningvenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Liaoningvenator (meaning "Liaoning hunter") is a genus of troodontid theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of China.

dinosaurs.name: Liliensternus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Liliensternus is an extinct genus of basal neotheropod dinosaur that lived approximately 210 million years ago during the latter part of the Triassic Period in what is now Germany.

dinosaurs.name: Limaysaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Limaysaurus ("Limay lizard") is a genus of rebbachisaurid sauropod dinosaurs which lived during the late Cretaceous period, about 99.7 to 94 million years ago, in the Cenomanian, in what is now South America (northwestern Patagonia).

dinosaurs.name: Limusaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Limusaurus is a genus of theropod dinosaur that lived in what is now China during the Late Jurassic, around 161 to 157 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Lingwulong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lingwulong is a genus of diplodocoid sauropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic of what is now Lingwu, Yinchuan, Ningxia, China.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Skeletal reconstruction and exemplar skeletal remains of Lingwulong shenqi

dinosaurs.name: Lingyuanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lingyuanosaurus is a genus of therizinosaurian theropod from the Early Cretaceous of Liaoning Province, China.

dinosaurs.name: Linhenykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Linhenykus is an extinct genus of alvarezsaurid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Inner Mongolia, China.

dinosaurs.name: Linheraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Linheraptor is a genus of dromaeosaurid dinosaur which lived in what is now China in the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Linhevenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Linhevenator is a genus of short-armed troodontid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) Bayan Mandahu Formation of Bayan Mandahu, Inner Mongolia, China.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Lirainosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lirainosaurus (meaning "slender lizard"; from the Basque lirain, meaning "slender", and the Greek sauros, meaning "lizard"[1]) is a genus of titanosaur sauropod which lived in what is now Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Lishulong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lishulong (Traditional Chinese: 栗樹龍 Simplified Chinese: 栗树龙 Pinyin: Lìshùlóng meaning "chestnut tree dragon") is a genus of sauropodiform dinosaur from the Early Jurassic (Sinemurian–Toarcian) Lufeng Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Lokiceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lokiceratops (meaning "Loki horned face") is an extinct genus of centrosaurine ceratopsian dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) Judith River Formation of Montana, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Lophorhothon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lophorhothon is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Alabama, and possibly Georgia and North Carolina.

dinosaurs.name: Loricatosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Loricatosaurus (meaning "armored lizard") is a genus of stegosaurid dinosaur known from Callovian-aged (Middle Jurassic) rocks of England and France.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Losillasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Losillasaurus (meaning "Losilla lizard") is a genus of turiasaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic and possibly Early Cretaceous (Kimmeridgian-?Berriasian) of southeastern Spain.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Museo de Ciencias Naturales de Valencia AD 11

dinosaurs.name: Lourinhanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lourinhanosaurus (meaning "Lourinhã lizard") was a genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Jurassic Period (Kimmeridgian/Tithonian) in Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Lourinhasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lourinhasaurus is an extinct genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur dating from Late Jurassic strata of Estremadura, Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Luanchuanraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Luanchuanraptor (meaning "Luanchuan thief") is a genus of dromaeosaurid theropod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous of China.

dinosaurs.name: Lucianovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lucianovenator is an extinct genus of coelophysid theropod dinosaur which lived in Argentina during the Triassic.

dinosaurs.name: Lufengosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lufengosaurus (Chinese: 祿豐龍 or 禄丰龙, meaning "Lufeng lizard") is a genus of massospondylid dinosaur which lived during the Early Jurassic period in what is now southwestern China.

dinosaurs.name: Lurdusaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lurdusaurus ("heavy lizard") is a genus of massive and unusually shaped iguanodont dinosaur from the Elrhaz Formation in Niger.

dinosaurs.name: Lusotitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lusotitan is a genus of herbivorous brachiosaurid sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic of Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Lusovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lusovenator (meaning "Portuguese hunter") is a genus of carcharodontosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic (Kimmeridgian) Praia de Amoreira Porto-Novo Member and the Late Jurassic-Early Cretaceous (Tithonian-Berriasian) Assenta Member of the Lourinhã Formation in present-day Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Lycorhinus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lycorhinus is a genus of heterodontosaurid ornithischian dinosaur from the Early Jurassic (Hettangian to Sinemurian ages) strata of the Elliot Formation located in the Cape Province, South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Lythronax
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Lythronax (LYE-thro-nax) is a genus of tyrannosaurid dinosaur that lived in North America around 81.9-81.5 million years ago during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Machairasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Machairasaurus is a genus of oviraptorid dinosaur which was found in the Bayan Mandahu Formation, China, dating to the late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Machairoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Machairoceratops (meaning "bent sword horned face"), previously known as the "Wahweap centrosaurine B", is an extinct genus of centrosaurine ceratopsian dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous Wahweap Formation (late Campanian stage) of Grand Staircase–Escalan

dinosaurs.name: Macrocollum
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Macrocollum is a genus of unaysaurid sauropodomorph dinosaur that lived during the Late Triassic period (early Norian) in what is now Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Macrogryphosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Macrogryphosaurus (meaning "big enigmatic lizard") is a genus of elasmarian dinosaur from the Coniacian age Upper Cretaceous Sierra Barrosa Formation (Neuquén Group) of Argentina in Patagonia.

dinosaurs.name: Macrurosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Macrurosaurus (meaning "large-tailed lizard") is the name given to a genus of dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Magnapaulia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Magnapaulia is a genus of herbivorous lambeosaurine hadrosaurid dinosaurs known from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) El Gallo Formation of Baja California, northwestern Mexico.

dinosaurs.name: Magnosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Magnosaurus (meaning 'large lizard') was a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic of England.

dinosaurs.name: Magyarosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Magyarosaurus ("Hungarian lizard") is a genus of dwarf sauropod dinosaur from late Cretaceous Period (early to late Maastrichtian) in Romania.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Titanosaur remains used to erect Magyarosaurus dacus

dinosaurs.name: Mahakala
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mahakala (from Sanskrit महाकाल,.mw-parser-output.IPA-label-small{font-size:85%}.mw-parser-output.references.IPA-label-small,.mw-parser-output.infobox.IPA-label-small,.mw-parser-output.navbox.IPA-label-small{font-size:100%}pronounced [mɐɦaːˈkaːlɐ]) is a genus of halszkaraptorine theropod dinosaur from the Campanian-age (about 80 million years ago) Upper Cretaceous Djadokhta Formation of Ömnögovi, Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Maiasaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Maiasaura (from Greek: μαῖα,[a] lit.

dinosaurs.name: Maip
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Maip is a genus of large megaraptorid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian) Chorrillo Formation of Santa Cruz, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Majungasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Majungasaurus (/məˌdʒʌŋɡəˈsɔːrəs/; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Malawisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Malawisaurus (meaning "Malawi lizard") is an extinct genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: August 1, 2012 - Malawisaurus on Display at the Royal Ontario Museum

dinosaurs.name: Malefica
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Malefica (meaning "witch" or "sorceress") is a genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) Aguja Formation of Texas.

dinosaurs.name: Maleriraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Maleriraptor is an extinct genus of herrerasaurian saurischian dinosaurs from the Late Triassic (Norian) Upper Maleri Formation of India.

dinosaurs.name: Mamenchisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mamenchisaurus (/məˌmʌntʃiˈsɔːrəs/ mə-MUN-chee-SOR-əs,[2] or spelling pronunciation /məˌmɛntʃɪˈsɔːrəs/) is an extinct genus of sauropod dinosaurs known for their remarkably long necks[3] which made up nearly half the total body length.[4] Numerous species have been assigned to the genus; however, the validity of these assignments has been questioned.

dinosaurs.name: Mandschurosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mandschurosaurus (meaning "lizard from Manchuria") is an extinct genus of hadrosaurids based on material from the Late Cretaceous of China and possibly also the Early Cretaceous of Laos.

dinosaurs.name: Manidens
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Manidens is an extinct genus of heterodontosaurid dinosaur from the Early Jurassic of Patagonia.

dinosaurs.name: Mansourasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mansourasaurus ("Mansoura lizard") is a genus of herbivorous lithostrotian sauropod dinosaur from the Quseir Formation of Egypt.

dinosaurs.name: Mantellisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Early Cretaceous, Barremian-early Aptian, 130-120 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Mantellisaurus is a genus of iguanodontian dinosaur that lived in the Barremian and early Aptian ages of the Early Cretaceous Period of Europe.

dinosaurs.name: Mapusaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mapusaurus (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Maraapunisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Maraapunisaurus is a dubious genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Morrison Formation of western North America.

dinosaurs.name: Marmarospondylus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Marmarospondylus ("marble [reference to the Forest Marble Formation] vertebra") is a dubious genus of sauropod dinosaur from Middle Jurassic deposits in the English Midlands.

dinosaurs.name: Marshosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Marshosaurus is a genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur, belonging to the family Piatnitzkysauridae, from the Late Jurassic Morrison Formation of Utah and possibly Colorado.[1]

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Marshosaurus bicentissimus skull cast - Natural History Museum of Utah - DSC07218

dinosaurs.name: Martharaptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Martharaptor is a genus of therizinosauroid theropod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous of the Cedar Mountain Formation in Utah.

dinosaurs.name: Masiakasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Masiakasaurus is a genus of small predatory theropod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous of Madagascar.

dinosaurs.name: Massospondylus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Massospondylus (/ˌmæsoʊˈspɒndɪləs/ MASS-oh-spon-di-lus[1]) is a genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Early Jurassic of southern Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Matheronodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Matheronodon (meaning "Matheron tooth") is a genus of rhabdodontid ornithopod dinosaur from the late Cretaceous Period of the Grès à Reptiles Formation in France.

dinosaurs.name: Maxakalisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Maxakalisaurus is a genus of titanosaur dinosaur, found in the Adamantina Formation of Brazil, in the state of Minas Gerais in 1998.

dinosaurs.name: Medusaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Medusaceratops is an extinct genus of centrosaurine ceratopsian dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous Judith River Formation (middle Campanian stage) of Montana, northern United States.

dinosaurs.name: Megalosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Megalosaurus (meaning "great lizard", from Greek μέγας, megas, meaning 'big', 'tall' or 'great' and σαῦρος, sauros, meaning 'lizard') is an extinct genus of large carnivorous theropod dinosaurs of the Middle Jurassic Epoch (Bathonian stage, 166 million years ago) of southern England.

dinosaurs.name: Megapnosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Megapnosaurus (meaning "big dead lizard", from Greek μέγα = "big", ἄπνοος = "not breathing", "dead", σαῦρος = "lizard") is an extinct genus of coelophysid theropod dinosaur that lived approximately 188 million years ago during the early part of the Jurassic Period in what is now Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Megaraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Megaraptor (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Melanorosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Melanorosaurus (meaning "Black Mountain Lizard", from the Greek melas/μέλας, "black", oros/ὄρος, "mountain" + sauros/σαῦρος, "lizard") is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur that lived during the Late Triassic period.

dinosaurs.name: Menefeeceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Menefeeceratops (meaning "Menefee Formation horned face") is a genus of ceratopsid dinosaur from the Menefee Formation in New Mexico, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Meraxes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Meraxes is a genus of large carcharodontosaurid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Huincul Formation of Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Mercuriceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mercuriceratops is an extinct genus of herbivorous chasmosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian stage) of Alberta, Canada and Montana, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Meroktenos
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Meroktenos is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur that lived during the Late Triassic period of what is now Lesotho.

dinosaurs.name: Metriacanthosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Metriacanthosaurus (meaning "moderately-spined lizard") is a genus of metriacanthosaurid dinosaur from the Oxford Clay Formation of England, dating to the Late Jurassic period, about 160 million years ago (lower Oxfordian).

dinosaurs.name: Mexidracon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mexidracon (meaning "Mexican dragon") is an extinct genus of ornithomimid theropod dinosaurs from the late Cretaceous Cerro del Pueblo Formation of Coahuila, Mexico.

dinosaurs.name: Microceratus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Microceratus (meaning "small-horned") is a genus of small ceratopsian dinosaur that lived in the Cretaceous period of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Microcoelus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Microcoelus (meaning "small hollow") is a dubious genus of small titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Santonian stage) Bajo de la Carpa Formation, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Micropachycephalosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Micropachycephalosaurus (meaning "small thick-headed lizard") is an extinct genus of basal marginocephalian dinosaur, containing only the type species, Micropachycephalosaurus hongtuyanensis.

dinosaurs.name: Microraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Microraptor (Greek, μικρός, mīkros: "small"; Latin, raptor: "one who seizes") is a genus of small, four-winged dromaeosaurid dinosaurs.

dinosaurs.name: Microvenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Microvenator (meaning "small hunter") is a genus of oviraptorosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Cloverly Formation in what is now south central Montana.

dinosaurs.name: Mierasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mierasaurus is an extinct genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of Utah, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Migmanychion
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Migmanychion (meaning "claw mixture") is an extinct genus of maniraptoran theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Longjiang Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Minimocursor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Minimocursor (meaning "smallest runner") is a genus of basal neornithischian dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Phu Kradung Formation of Thailand.

dinosaurs.name: Minmi
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Minmi is a genus of small herbivorous ankylosaurian dinosaur that lived during the early Cretaceous Period of Australia, about 120 to 112 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Minotaurasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Minotaurasaurus (meaning "Minos'-bull reptile") is a monospecific genus of ankylosaurid dinosaur that lived in Mongolia during the Late Cretaceous (late Campanian stage, ~75-71 Ma) in what is now the Djadochta Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Minqaria
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Minqaria (meaning "beak") is a genus of lambeosaurine dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian) Ouled Abdoun Basin of Morocco.

dinosaurs.name: Mirischia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mirischia is an extinct genus of coelurosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Albian stage (Early Cretaceous Period) of northeastern Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Mnyamawamtuka
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mnyamawamtuka (.mw-parser-output.IPA-label-small{font-size:85%}.mw-parser-output.references.IPA-label-small,.mw-parser-output.infobox.IPA-label-small,.mw-parser-output.navbox.IPA-label-small{font-size:100%}Swahili pronunciation: [m̩ˌɲamawam̩ˈtukə];[1] meaning "beast of the Mtuka river drainage" in Kiswahili) is a genus of lithostrotian titanosaur sauropod dinosaur from the Cretaceous Galula Formation in Tanzania.

dinosaurs.name: Moabosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Moabosaurus (meaning "Moab reptile") is a genus of turiasaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Cedar Mountain Formation of Utah, United States.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Mochlodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mochlodon is a genus of rhabdodontid dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous of Austria and Hungary.

dinosaurs.name: Mongolosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mongolosaurus is a genus of titanosauriform sauropod dinosaur which lived during the Early Cretaceous of China.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Mongolostegus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mongolostegus (meaning "Mongolian roof") is an extinct genus of stegosaurian dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous (Aptian–Albian ages) Dzunbain Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Monoclonius
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Monoclonius (meaning "single sprout") is an extinct dubious genus of herbivorous ceratopsian dinosaur found in the Late Cretaceous layers of the Judith River Formation in Montana, United States, and the uppermost rock layers of the Dinosaur Park Formation in A

dinosaurs.name: Monolophosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Monolophosaurus (/ˌmɒnoʊˌlɒfoʊˈsɔːrəs/ MON-oh-LOF-oh-SOR-əs;[1] meaning "single-crested lizard") is an extinct genus of tetanuran theropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Shishugou Formation in what is now Xinjiang, China.[2][3] It was named for the single crest on top of its skull.

dinosaurs.name: Mononykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mononykus (/məˈnɒnɪkəs/ mə-NON-ik-əs, sometimes /ˌmɒnoʊˈnaɪkəs/ MON-oh-NY-kəs; meaning "one claw") is a genus of alvarezsaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous in what is now Mongolia, specifically the Nemegt Formation, about 70 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Montanoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Montanoceratops /mɒnˌtænoʊˈsɛrətɒps/ is an extinct genus of small ceratopsian dinosaur that lived approximately 70 million years ago during the latter part of the Cretaceous Period in what is now Montana and Alberta.

dinosaurs.name: Morelladon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Morelladon is an extinct genus of herbivorous styracosternan ornithopod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Morinosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Morinosaurus (meaning "Morini lizard", for an ancient people of northern France) is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from an unnamed formation of Kimmeridgian-aged (Late Jurassic) rocks from Boulogne-sur-Mer, Département du Pas-de-Calais, France.

dinosaurs.name: Moros
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Moros is a genus of small tyrannosauroid theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period in what is now Utah.

dinosaurs.name: Morrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Morrosaurus is an extinct genus of herbivorous elasmarian dinosaur that lived in the late Cretaceous in Antarctica.

dinosaurs.name: Mosaiceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mosaiceratops is a genus of ceratopsian dinosaur that lived in the upper Cretaceous Xiaguan Formation in what is now the Henan Province of China.

dinosaurs.name: Murusraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Murusraptor ("wall thief") is a genus of carnivorous megaraptoran theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Coniacian age) Sierra Barrosa Formation, part of the Neuquén Group of Patagonia, in Argentina, South America.

dinosaurs.name: Musankwa
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Musankwa is an extinct genus of massopodan sauropodomorph dinosaurs from the Late Triassic (Norian) Pebbly Arkose Formation of Zimbabwe.

dinosaurs.name: Mussaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mussaurus (meaning "mouse lizard") is a genus of herbivorous sauropodomorph dinosaur that lived in southern Argentina during the Sinemurian stage of the Early Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Muttaburrasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Muttaburrasaurus was a genus of herbivorous iguanodontian ornithopod dinosaur that lived in what is now northeastern Australia sometime between 112 and 103 million years ago[1] during the early Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Mymoorapelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Mymoorapelta (Meaning "Shield from the Mygatt-Moore Quarry" after a combination of the names of the discoverers of the Mygatt-Moore Quarry that fossils were originally collected from, and the Greek word πέλτα, meaning "shield") is a nodosaurid ankylosaur from the Late Jurassic (Kimmeridgian-Tithonian, around 155 to 150 million years ago) Morrison Formation (Brushy Basin Member) of western Colorado and central Utah, USA.

dinosaurs.name: Naashoibitosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Naashoibitosaurus (from Navajo naʼashǫ́ʼii bitooh—"creek lizard") is a genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur that lived about 73 million years ago, in the Late Cretaceous, and was found in the Kirtland Formation of the San Juan Basin in New Mexico, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Nankangia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nankangia is an extinct genus of caenagnathoid oviraptorosaurian dinosaur known from the Upper Cretaceous Nanxiong Formation of Nankang County, Ganzhou City of Jiangxi Province, southeastern China.

dinosaurs.name: Nanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nanosaurus ("small or dwarf lizard") is an extinct genus of neornithischian dinosaur that lived about 155 to 148 million years ago, during the Late Jurassic in North America.

dinosaurs.name: Nanotyrannus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nanotyrannus (/ˈnænoʊˌtaɪˌrænəs, -tɪ- /, NAN-o-ti-RAN-us) is a genus of tyrannosauroid dinosaur that lived in what is now western North America during the Maastrichtian age of the Late Cretaceous, 67 to 66 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Nanshiungosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nanshiungosaurus (meaning "Nanxiong's lizard") is a genus of therizinosaurid that lived in what is now Asia during the Late Cretaceous of South China.

dinosaurs.name: Nanuqsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nanuqsaurus (meaning "polar bear lizard") is a genus of tyrannosaurine theropod dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous (Latest Campanian age) Prince Creek Formation of the North Slope of Alaska.

dinosaurs.name: Nanyangosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nanyangosaurus is a genus of herbivorous ornithopod dinosaur belonging to the Hadrosauromorpha that lived in the Late Cretaceous of present-day Henan Province, China.

dinosaurs.name: Narindasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Narindasaurus (meaning "Narinda Bay lizard") is a genus of turiasaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Isalo III Formation of Madagascar.

dinosaurs.name: Nasutoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nasutoceratops is a genus of ceratopsid dinosaur that lived in North America during the Late Cretaceous period, about 76.0–75.5 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Natovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Natovenator is a genus of halszkaraptorine dromaeosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Barun Goyot Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Navajoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Navajoceratops (meaning "Navajo horned face") is a genus of ceratopsid dinosaur from the late Cretaceous Period of what is now North America.

dinosaurs.name: Nebulasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nebulasaurus is an extinct genus of basal eusauropod dinosaur known from the early Middle Jurassic Zhanghe Formation (Aalenian or Bajocian stage) of Yunnan Province, China.

dinosaurs.name: Nedcolbertia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nedcolbertia is a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Valanginian-aged) Cedar Mountain Formation of Utah.

dinosaurs.name: Nedoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nedoceratops (meaning "insufficient horned face") is a controversial genus of ceratopsid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous period Lance Formation of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Neimongosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Neimongosaurus (meaning "Nei Mongol lizard") is a genus of herbivorous therizinosaurian theropod dinosaur that lived in China during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Nemegtomaia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nemegtomaia is a genus of oviraptorid dinosaur from what is now Mongolia that lived in the Late Cretaceous Period, about 72 to 70 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Nemegtonykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nemegtonykus (meaning "Nemegt claw") is a genus of alvarezsaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (early Maastrichtian) Nemegt Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Nemegtosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nemegtosaurus (meaning "reptile from the Nemegt") was a sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Period of what is now Mongolia.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Opisthocoelicaudia Museum of Evolution in Warsaw 14

dinosaurs.name: Neosodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Neosodon (meaning "new tooth") was a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the late Tithonian-aged (Upper Jurassic) Sables et Gres a Trigonia gibbosa of Pas-de-Calais department, France.[1][2] It has never been formally given a species name, but is often seen as N. praecursor, which actually comes from a different animal.

dinosaurs.name: Neovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Neovenator (nˈiːə͡ʊvˌɛne͡ɪtə; "new hunter") is a genus of carcharodontosaurian theropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Neuquenraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Neuquenraptor (meaning Neuquén thief) is a genus of unenlagiine theropod dinosaurs that lived in South America during the Late Cretaceous in what is now the Portezuelo Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Neuquensaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Neuquensaurus (meaning "Neuquén lizard") is a genus of saltasaurid sauropod dinosaur that lived in the Late Cretaceous, about 80 million years ago in Argentina in South America.

dinosaurs.name: Nevadadromeus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nevadadromeus (meaning "Nevada runner") is an extinct genus of small thescelosaurine ornithischian dinosaur, from the Willow Tank Formation of Nevada, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Newtonsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Newtonsaurus is an extinct genus of possibly coelophysoid theropod dinosaur from the Late Triassic (Rhaetian) Lilstock Formation of South Wales, Great Britain.

dinosaurs.name: Ngwevu
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ngwevu is a genus of massospondylid sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Lower Jurassic of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Nhandumirim
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nhandumirim (meaning "small rhea" in the Tupi language) is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Carnian age of Late Triassic Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Nigersaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nigersaurus (/niːˈʒɛərsɔːrəs, ˈnaɪdʒərsɔːrəs/) is a genus of rebbachisaurid sauropod dinosaur that lived during the middle Cretaceous period, about 115 to 105 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Niobrarasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Niobrarasaurus (meaning "Niobrara lizard") is an extinct genus of nodosaurid ankylosaurian dinosaur which lived during the Late Cretaceous, around 87 to 82 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Nipponosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nipponosaurus (meaning "Japanese lizard") is a lambeosaurine hadrosaur from sediments of the Yezo Group, in Sinegorsk on the island of Sakhalin, which was part of Japan at the time of the species' classification.

dinosaurs.name: Noasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Noasaurus ("Northwestern Argentina lizard") is a genus of ceratosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Maastrichtian (Late Cretaceous) of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Nodocephalosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nodocephalosaurus (meaning "knob headed lizard") is a monospecific genus of ankylosaurid dinosaur from New Mexico that lived during the Late Cretaceous (late Campanian to early Maastrichtian stage, 73.49 to 72 Ma) in what is now the De-na-zin member of the Kirtland Formation.[1][2] The type and only species, Nodocephalosaurus kirtlandensis, is known only from a partial skull.[1] It was named in 1999 by Robert M.

dinosaurs.name: Nodosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nodosaurus (meaning 'knobbed lizard') is a genus of herbivorous nodosaurid ankylosaurian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous, the fossils of which are found exclusively in the Frontier Formation in Wyoming.

dinosaurs.name: Nomingia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nomingia is a genus of oviraptorosaurian theropod dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous Bugin Tsav Beds of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Nopcsaspondylus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nopcsaspondylus (meaning "Nopcsa's vertebra", in reference to the original describer) is a genus of rebbachisaurid sauropod dinosaur (a type of large, long-necked, quadrupedal herbivorous dinosaur) from the Cenomanian-aged (Upper Cretaceous) Candeleros Formation of Neuquén, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Notatesseraeraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Notatesseraeraptor ("feature mosaic tile thief"; from the Latin "nota", feature; "tesserae", tiles used to make a mosaic, in reference to the mixture of features normally found on dilophosaurids and coelophysoids; and "raptor", thief) is a genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Triassic of what is now Switzerland.

dinosaurs.name: Nothronychus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nothronychus (meaning "slothful claw") is a genus of therizinosaurid theropod dinosaurs that lived in North America during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Notoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Notoceratops (meaning "southern horned face") is a dubious genus of extinct ornithischian dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Notocolossus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Notocolossus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from late Cretaceous strata of Mendoza Province, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Notohypsilophodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Notohypsilophodon (meaning "southern Hypsilophodon") is a genus of ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Nqwebasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nqwebasaurus is a probable ornithomimosaurian theropod from the Early Cretaceous of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Nullotitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nullotitan (meaning "Nullo's giant", in honor of paleontologist Francisco Nullo) is an extinct genus of lithostrotian titanosaur dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous Chorrillo Formation of Santa Cruz Province, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Nuthetes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Nuthetes is a genus of coelurosaurian theropod dinosaur, either a dromaeosaurid or tyrannosauroid, known only from fossil teeth and jaw fragments found in rocks of the middle Berriasian age (Early Cretaceous) in the Cherty Freshwater Member of the Lulworth Formation in England and also the Angeac-Charente bonebed in France.

dinosaurs.name: Obelignathus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Cretaceous, upper Campanian-lower Maastrichtian
dinosaurs.description: Obelignathus (meaning "Obelix jaw") is an extinct genus of rhabdodontomorph ornithopod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous 'Grès à Reptiles' Formation of France.

dinosaurs.name: Oblitosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Oblitosaurus (meaning "forgotten lizard") is a genus of ankylopollexian ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic Villar del Arzobispo Formation of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Oceanotitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Oceanotitan (meaning "ocean giant") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur known from the Upper Jurassic (latest Kimmeridgian stage, about 149 million years ago) Lourinhã Formation of Portugal.

dinosaurs.name: Ohmdenosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ohmdenosaurus ('Ohmden lizard') is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived during the Early Jurassic epoch in what is now Germany.

dinosaurs.name: Ojoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ojoceratops ( meaning "Ojo Alamo horned face") is a genus of ceratopsian dinosaur which lived in what is now New Mexico, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Ojoraptorsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ojoraptorsaurus is a dubious genus of oviraptorosaurian dinosaur from the late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Olorotitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Olorotitan is a monotypic genus of lambeosaurine duck-billed dinosaur, containing a single species, O. arharensis.

dinosaurs.name: Omeisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Omeisaurus (meaning "Omei lizard") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Middle to Late Jurassic Period (Bathonian-Oxfordian stage) of what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Ondogurvel
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ondogurvel (meaning "egg lizard") is a genus of alvarezsaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) Barun Goyot Formation in southern Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Oohkotokia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Oohkotokia ( OH-oh-kə-TOH-kee-ə) is a genus of ankylosaurid dinosaur within the subfamily Ankylosaurinae.

dinosaurs.name: Opisthocoelicaudia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Opisthocoelicaudia is a genus of sauropod dinosaur of the Late Cretaceous Period discovered in the Gobi Desert of Mongolia.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Opisthocoelicaudia Museum of Evolution in Warsaw 02

dinosaurs.name: Oplosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Oplosaurus (meaning "armed or weapon lizard" or "armoured lizard"; see below for discussion) was a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Lower Cretaceous (Barremian stage) Wessex Formation of the Isle of Wight, England.

dinosaurs.name: Orkoraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Orkoraptor is a genus of medium-sized megaraptoran theropod dinosaur from the late Cretaceous Period of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Ornatops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ornatops (meaning "ornate face") is a genus of brachylophosaurin saurolophine hadrosaur from the Late Cretaceous Menefee Formation of New Mexico, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Ornitholestes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ornitholestes (from Ancient Greek ὄρνις (órnis), meaning "bird", and λῃστής (lēistḗs), meaning "robber", and thus, "bird robber") is a small theropod dinosaur of the late Jurassic (Brushy Basin Member of the Morrison Formation, middle Kimmeridgian age, about 1

dinosaurs.name: Ornithomimoides
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ornithomimoides ("bird mimic-like") is a dubious genus of theropod dinosaur, from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian stage, sometime between 70 and 66 mya) Lameta Formation of India.

dinosaurs.name: Ornithomimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ornithomimus (/ˌɔːrnɪθəˈmaɪməs, -θoʊ-/;[2] "bird mimic") is a genus of ornithomimid theropod dinosaurs from the Campanian and Maastrichtian ages of the Late Cretaceous in western North America.

dinosaurs.name: Ornithopsis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ornithopsis (meaning "bird-likeness") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of England and possibly Germany.

dinosaurs.name: Ornithotarsus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ornithotarsus (lit.

dinosaurs.name: Orodromeus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Orodromeus (meaning "Mountain Runner") is a genus of herbivorous orodromine thescelosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Orthomerus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Orthomerus (meaning "straight femur") is a genus of dubious hadrosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of the Netherlands.

dinosaurs.name: Oryctodromeus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Oryctodromeus (from Ancient Greek ὀρυκτήρ (oruktḗs), meaning "digger", and δρομαῖος (dromaîos), meaning "runner") is an extinct genus of small orodromine thescelosaurid dinosaur.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Oryctodromeus cubicularis - Museum of the Rockies

dinosaurs.name: Ostafrikasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ostafrikasaurus is a potentially dubious genus of theropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic period of what is now Lindi Region, Tanzania.

dinosaurs.name: Ostromia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ostromia (named after John Ostrom) is a genus of anchiornithid theropod dinosaur containing a single species, Ostromia crassipes.

dinosaurs.name: Ouranosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ouranosaurus is a genus of herbivorous basal hadrosauriform dinosaur that lived during the Aptian stage of the Early Cretaceous of modern-day Niger and Cameroon.

dinosaurs.name: Overoraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Overoraptor (/ˌʊuvərʊuˈræptər/, meaning "piebald thief") is an extinct genus of paravian theropod of uncertain affinities from the Late Cretaceous Huincul Formation of Argentinian Patagonia.

dinosaurs.name: Overosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Overosaurus (meaning "Overo lizard", after the Cerro Overo locality[1]) is an extinct genus of sauropod dinosaurs, containing only a single species, Overosaurus paradasorum.

dinosaurs.name: Oviraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Oviraptor (/ˈoʊvɪræptər/; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Owenodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Owenodon is a genus of iguanodontian dinosaur known from a partial lower jaw discovered in Early Cretaceous-aged rocks of Dorset, United Kingdom, and possibly also Romania and Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Oxalaia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Oxalaia (in reference to the African deity Oxalá) is a controversial genus of spinosaurid dinosaur that lived in what is now the Northeast Region of Brazil during the Cenomanian stage of the Late Cretaceous period, sometime between 100.5 and 93.9 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Ozraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
