dinosaurs.description: Ozraptor (meaning "Australian thief") is a genus of possibly abelisauroid theropod dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic (Bajocian) Colalura Sandstone[1] of Australia, known from fragmentary remains.

dinosaurs.name: Pachycephalosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pachycephalosaurus (/ˌpækɪˌsɛfələˈsɔːrəs/;[2] meaning "thick-headed lizard", from Greek pachys/παχύς "thickness", kephalon/κεφαλή "head" and sauros/σαῦρος "lizard")[3] is a genus of pachycephalosaurid ornithischian dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Pachyrhinosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pachyrhinosaurus (from Ancient Greek Παχυ (pachy), thick; ρινό (rinó), nose; and σαυρος (sauros), lizard)[1] is a genus of centrosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous period of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Padillasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Padillasaurus is an extinct genus of titanosauriform sauropod known from the Early Cretaceous (Barremian stage) Paja Formation in Colombia.

dinosaurs.name: Palaeoscincus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Palaeoscincus (meaning "ancient lizard" from Greek: παλαιός palaios and Greek: σκίγγος skinkos)[1] is a dubious genus of ankylosaurian dinosaur based on teeth from the mid-late Campanian-age Upper Cretaceous Judith River Formation of Montana.[2] Like several other dinosaur genera named by Joseph Leidy (Deinodon, Thespesius, and Trachodon), it is an historically important genus with a convoluted taxonomy that has been all but abandoned by modern dinosaur paleontologists.

dinosaurs.name: Paludititan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Paludititan is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur which lived in the area of present Romania during the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Pamparaptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pamparaptor (, meaning "thief of the Pampas") is an extinct genus of paravian theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Portezuelo Formation of the Neuquén province in Argentine Patagonia.

dinosaurs.name: Panguraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Panguraptor ("Pangu [a Chinese god] plunderer") is a genus of coelophysid theropod dinosaur known from fossils discovered in Lower Jurassic rocks of southern China.

dinosaurs.name: Panoplosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Panoplosaurus is a genus of armoured dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Panphagia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Panphagia is a genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur described in 2009.

dinosaurs.name: Pantydraco
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pantydraco (where "panty-" is short for Pant-y-ffynnon, signifying hollow of the spring/well in Welsh, referring to the quarry at Bonvilston in South Wales where it was found) is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Late Triassic of the United Kingdom (Wales).

dinosaurs.name: Papiliovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Papiliovenator (meaning "butterfly hunter", after a butterfly-shaped feature on its first two dorsal vertebrae) is a genus of troodontid theropod dinosaur from the Bayan Mandahu Formation of Inner Mongolia, China.

dinosaurs.name: Paralitherizinosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Paralitherizinosaurus is an extinct genus of therizinosaurid theropod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous Osoushinai Formation of Hokkaido, Japan.

dinosaurs.name: Paralititan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Paralititan (meaning "tidal giant") is an extinct genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur that lived in present-day Egypt during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Paranthodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Paranthodon ( pə-RAN-thə-don) is a genus of stegosaurian dinosaur that lived in what is now South Africa during the Early Cretaceous, between 139 and 131 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Pararhabdodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pararhabdodon (meaning "near fluted tooth" in reference to Rhabdodon) is a genus of tsintaosaurin hadrosaurid dinosaur from the Maastrichtian-aged (Late Cretaceous) Tremp Group of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Parasaurolophus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Parasaurolophus (; meaning "beside crested lizard" in reference to Saurolophus) is a genus of hadrosaurid "duck-billed" dinosaur that lived in what is now western North America and possibly Asia during the Late Cretaceous period, about 76.5–73 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Paraxenisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Paraxenisaurus (, meaning "strange lizard") is an extinct genus of ornithomimosaurian theropod from the Late Cretaceous Cerro del Pueblo Formation of Coahuila in Mexico.

dinosaurs.name: Pareisactus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pareisactus (from the Greek "pareisaktos", meaning "intruder", referring to being represented as a single element among hundreds of hadrosaurid bones) is a genus of rhabdodontid ornithopod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (lower Maastrichtian) Conquès Member of the Tremp Formation in the Southern Pyrenees of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Parksosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Parksosaurus (meaning "William Parks's lizard") is a genus of neornithischian dinosaur from the early Maastrichtian-aged (Upper Cretaceous) Horseshoe Canyon Formation of Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Paronychodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Paronychodon (meaning "beside claw tooth") was a theropod dinosaur genus.

dinosaurs.name: Parvicursor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Parvicursor (meaning "small runner") is a genus of very small alvarezsaurid maniraptoran dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Patagonykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Patagonykus (meaning "Patagonian claw") is a genus of alvarezsauroid theropod dinosaur from the Upper Cretaceous of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Patagopelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Patagopelta (meaning "Patagonian shield") is an extinct genus of ankylosaurian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (upper Campanian–lower Maastrichtian) Allen Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Patagosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Patagosaurus (meaning "Patagonia lizard"[1]) is an extinct genus of eusauropod dinosaur from the middle Toarcian of Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Patagotitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Patagotitan is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Albian stage) Cerro Barcino Formation in Chubut Province, Patagonia, Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Pawpawsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pawpawsaurus, meaning "Pawpaw Lizard", is a nodosaurid ankylosaur from the Cretaceous (late Albian) of Tarrant County, Texas, discovered in May 1992.

dinosaurs.name: Pectinodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pectinodon is a genus of troodontid theropod dinosaurs from the end of the Maastrichtian age of the Late Cretaceous period (66 mya).

dinosaurs.name: Pedopenna
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pedopenna (/ˌpɛdoʊˈpɛnə/) (meaning "foot feather") is a genus of small, feathered, maniraptoran dinosaur from the Daohugou Beds in China.

dinosaurs.name: Pegomastax
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pegomastax is a genus of heterodontosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Early Jurassic of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Pelecanimimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pelecanimimus (meaning "pelican mimic") is an extinct genus of basal ("primitive") ornithomimosaurian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Pellegrinisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pellegrinisaurus (meaning Lizard from Pelligrini) is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur that lived in South America during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Peloroplites
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Peloroplites (meaning "monstrous heavy one") is a monospecific genus of nodosaurid dinosaur from Utah that lived during the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian to lower Turonian stage, 98.2 to 93 Ma) in what is now the Mussentuchit Member of the Cedar Mountain Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Pelorosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pelorosaurus (/pəˌlɒroʊˈsɔːrəs/ pə-LORR-oh-SOR-əs; meaning "monstrous lizard") is a genus of titanosauriform sauropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Pendraig
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pendraig (meaning "chief dragon" in Middle Welsh) is a genus of coelophysoid theropod dinosaur from South Wales.

dinosaurs.name: Pentaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pentaceratops ("five-horned face") is a genus of herbivorous ceratopsid dinosaur from the late Cretaceous Period of what is now North America.

dinosaurs.name: Perijasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Perijasaurus (meaning "Perija lizard") is a genus of basal eusauropod sauropod dinosaur from the "Girón-type redbeds" of the La Quinta Formation of Cesar Department, north-eastern Colombia.

dinosaurs.name: Petrobrasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Petrobrasaurus is a genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Petrustitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Petrustitan is a eutitanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian) of Romania.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Paralectotype and lectotype of Petrustitan hungaricus

dinosaurs.name: Phaedrolosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Phaedrolosaurus (meaning "elated lizard") is a dubious genus of coelurosaurian (perhaps dromaeosaurid) theropod dinosaur, based on an isolated and non-diagnostic tooth from the Early Cretaceous Lianmuqin Formation of Wuerho, in the autonomous region of Xinjian

dinosaurs.name: Philovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Philovenator (literally meaning "love hunter") is an extinct genus of troodontid paravian dinosaurs from the Wulansuhai Formation (dated to the Campanian age, sometime between 75 and 71 million years ago) of Inner Mongolia, China.

dinosaurs.name: Phuwiangosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Phuwiangosaurus (meaning "Phu Wiang lizard") is a genus of titanosauriform dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Valanginian-Hauterivian) Sao Khua Formation of Thailand.

dinosaurs.name: Phuwiangvenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Phuwiangvenator (/fuˈwɛŋvɪnɑːtər/) is an extinct genus of megaraptoran theropod that lived during the Early Cretaceous period in what is now Thailand.

dinosaurs.name: Phyllodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Phyllodon (meaning "leaf tooth") is a genus of small ornithischian dinosaur from the Late Jurassic (Kimmeridgian) Camadas de Guimarota Formation of Leiria, Portugal and possibly also the Middle Jurassic (Bathonian) Chipping Norton Limestone of England.[1] It may have been closely related to contemporaneous ornithischian dinosaurs from North America.

dinosaurs.name: Piatnitzkysaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Piatnitzkysaurus (/ˌpiːətnɪtskiːˈsɔːrəs/ pee-ət-NITS-kee-SOR-əs; meaning "Piatnitzky's lizard") is a genus of tetanuran theropod dinosaur that lived approximately 179 to 177 million years ago during the lower part of the Jurassic Period in what is now Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Pilmatueia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pilmatueia is a genus of diplodocoid sauropods belonging to the family Dicraeosauridae that lived during the Early Cretaceous in what is now Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Pinacosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pinacosaurus (meaning "Plank lizard") is a genus of ankylosaurid thyreophoran dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous (Campanian, roughly 75 to 71 million years ago), mainly in Mongolia and China.

dinosaurs.name: Pisanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pisanosaurus (/pɪˌsænəˈsɔːrəs/ piss-AN-ə-SOR-əs) is an extinct genus of early dinosauriform, likely an ornithischian or silesaurid, from the Late Triassic of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Piveteausaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Piveteausaurus (meaning "Jean Piveteau's lizard") is a genus of theropod dinosaur known from a partial skull discovered in the Middle Jurassic (164.7 to 161.2 million years ago) Marnes de Dives of Calvados, northern France.

dinosaurs.name: Plateosauravus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Plateosauravus ("grandfather of Plateosaurus") is a basal plateosaurian of uncertain affinities from the Late Triassic Elliot Formation of South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Plateosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Plateosaurus (probably meaning "broad lizard", often mistranslated as "flat lizard") is a genus of plateosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Triassic epoch of the Triassic period, around 214 to 204 million years ago, in what is now Central and Northern Europe.

dinosaurs.name: Platypelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Platypelta is an extinct genus of herbivorous ankylosaurid dinosaurs within the subfamily Ankylosaurinae.

dinosaurs.name: Platytholus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Platytholus is an extinct genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian) Hell Creek Formation of the United States.

dinosaurs.name: Pneumatoraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pneumatoraptor is a genus of small paravian, possibly a dromaeosaurid, dinosaur that lived in Hungary.

dinosaurs.name: Podokesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Podokesaurus is a genus of coelophysoid dinosaur that lived in what is now the eastern United States during the Early Jurassic Period.

dinosaurs.name: Poekilopleuron
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Poekilopleuron (meaning "varied ribs") is a genus of carnosaurian theropod dinosaur, which lived during the middle Bathonian of the Jurassic, about 168 to 166 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Polacanthus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Polacanthus (from the Ancient Greek polys-/πολύς- "many" and akantha/ἄκανθα "thorn" or "prickle") is an extinct genus of ankylosaurian dinosaurs from the early Cretaceous (130–125 million years ago) of England.

dinosaurs.name: Portellsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Portellsaurus (meaning "Portell Lizard") is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur from the Lower Cretaceous Margas de Mirambell Formation of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Prenocephale
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Prenocephale (meaning "sloping head"[1]) is a genus of small pachycephalosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Nemegt Formation of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Priconodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Priconodon (meaning "saw cone tooth"[1]) is an extinct genus of ankylosaurian dinosaur (perhaps nodosaurid), mainly known from its large teeth.

dinosaurs.name: Proa
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Proa is a genus of basal styracosternan iguanodont known from the Early Cretaceous Escucha Formation (lower Albian stage) of Teruel Province, Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Probactrosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Probactrosaurus (meaning "before Bactrosaurus") is an extinct genus of basal hadrosauroid iguanodontian dinosaur that lived in China during the Early Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Probrachylophosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Probrachylophosaurus is a genus of large herbivorous brachylophosaurin hadrosaurid dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) Judith River Formation of Montana and the Foremost Formation of Alberta.[1] The type (and only named) species is Probrachylophosaurus bergei.

dinosaurs.name: Proceratosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Proceratosaurus (/proʊsəˌrætoʊˈsɔːrəs/ proh-sə-RAT-oh-SAW-rəs[2]) is a genus of theropod dinosaur that lived during the Middle Jurassic in what is now England.

dinosaurs.name: Procompsognathus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Procompsognathus /ˌproʊkɒmpˈsɒɡnəθəs/ is an extinct genus of coelophysid theropod dinosaur that lived approximately 210 million years ago during the later part of the Triassic Period, in what is now Germany.

dinosaurs.name: Propanoplosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Propanoplosaurus is a genus of herbivorous nodosaurid dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Patuxent Formation of Maryland, USA.

dinosaurs.name: Prosaurolophus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Prosaurolophus (/ˌproʊsɔːˈrɒləfəs/; meaning "before Saurolophus", in comparison to the later dinosaur with a similar head crest) is a genus of hadrosaurid (or duck-billed) dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of North America.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Prosaurolophus maximus, Red Deer River, Alberta, collected 1921 by Levi Sternberg - Royal Ontario Museum - DSC09845

dinosaurs.name: Protarchaeopteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Protarchaeopteryx (meaning "before Archaeopteryx" although it is from the Cretaceous and Archaeopteryx is from the Jurassic) is a genus of turkey-sized feathered theropod dinosaur from China.[1] Known from the Jianshangou bed of the Yixian Formation, it lived during the early Aptian age of the Early Cretaceous, approximately 124.6 million years ago.[2] It was probably a herbivore or omnivore, although its hands were very similar to those of small carnivorous dinosaurs.

dinosaurs.name: Protathlitis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Protathlitis (meaning "champion") is a potentially dubious and chimeric genus of theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Barremian) Arcillas de Morella Formation of Castellón, Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Protoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Protoceratops (/ˌproʊtoʊˈsɛrətɒps/; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Psittacosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Psittacosaurus (/ˌsɪtəkəˈsɔːrəs/ SIT-ə-kə-SOR-əs; "parrot lizard")[1] is a genus of extinct ceratopsian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of what is now Asia, existing between 125 and 105 million years ago.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: The Childrens Museum of Indianapolis - Psittacosaurus skeleton cast

dinosaurs.name: Pterospondylus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pterospondylus (meaning "winged vertebra") is a dubious genus of theropod dinosaur from the Late Triassic.

dinosaurs.name: Puertasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Puertasaurus is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived in South America during the Late Cretaceous Period.

dinosaurs.name: Pukyongosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pukyongosaurus (meaning "Pukyong lizard", after the Pukyong National University[1]) is a genus of titanosauriform dinosaur that lived in South Korea during the Early Cretaceous Period (Aptian - Albian).

dinosaurs.name: Pulanesaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pulanesaura is an extinct genus of basal sauropodiform known from the Early Jurassic (late Hettangian to Sinemurian) Upper Elliot Formation of the Free State, South Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Pulaosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pulaosaurus (meaning "Pulao lizard") is an extinct genus of neornithischian dinosaur from the mid-Jurassic (Callovian–Oxfordian ages) Tiaojishan Formation of Hebei, China.

dinosaurs.name: Pycnonemosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pycnonemosaurus (meaning 'dense forest lizard') is a genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur that belonged to the family Abelisauridae.

dinosaurs.name: Pyroraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Pyroraptor (meaning "fire thief") is an extinct genus of paravian dinosaur, probably a dromaeosaurid or unenlagiid, from the Late Cretaceous Ibero-Armorican island, of what is now southern France.

dinosaurs.name: Qantassaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Qantassaurus ( KWAHN-tə-SOR-əs) is a genus of basal two-legged, plant-eating elasmarian ornithischian dinosaur that lived in Australia about 125-112 million years ago, when the continent was still partly south of the Antarctic Circle.

dinosaurs.name: Qianjiangsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Qianjiangsaurus (meaning "Qianjiang lizard") is an extinct genus of hadrosauroidean ornithopod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous Zhengyang Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Qianzhousaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Qianzhousaurus (meaning "Qianzhou lizard") is a genus of tyrannosaurid dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Maastrichtian age of the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Qiaowanlong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Qiaowanlong (meaning "Qiaowan dragon") is an extinct genus of sauropod dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Qiupalong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Qiupalong (IPA: /ˌtɕʰuˈpaːloŋ/; Chinese: 秋扒龙; pinyin: Qiūpálóng; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Quaesitosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Quaesitosaurus (meaning "extraordinary lizard") is a genus of nemegtosaurid sauropod containing only the type species, Q. orientalis, described in 1983.

dinosaurs.name: Quetecsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Quetecsaurus (meaning "fire lizard", from the Milcayac word "quetec") is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur known from the Late Cretaceous of the southern Mendoza Province, western Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Quilmesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Quilmesaurus is a genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur from the Upper Cretaceous (Campanian to Maastrichtian stages) Allen Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Qunkasaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Cretaceous, late Campanian-early Maastrichtian
dinosaurs.description: Qunkasaura is a genus of saltasauroid titanosaur dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian–Maastrichtian) Lo Hueco site of the Villalba de la Sierra Formation of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Rahiolisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rahiolisaurus is a genus of abelisaurid theropod dinosaur which existed in India during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Rahonavis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rahonavis is a genus of bird-like theropod from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian, from about 70 to 66 mya) of what is now northwestern Madagascar.

dinosaurs.name: Rajasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rajasaurus (lit.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Exhibit of Rajasaurus Narmadensis at Regional Museum of Natural History,Bhopal,India 2

dinosaurs.name: Rapator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rapator is a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Griman Creek Formation of New South Wales, Australia, dating to the Cenomanian age of the Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Rapetosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rapetosaurus (/rəˌpeɪtuːˈsɔːrəs/ rə-PAY-too-SOR-əs[2]) is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur that lived in Madagascar from 70 to 66 million years ago, at the end of the Cretaceous Period.[1] Only one species, Rapetosaurus krausei, has been identified.

dinosaurs.name: Raptorex
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Raptorex is a genus of tyrannosaurid dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Ratchasimasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ratchasimasaurus (meaning "Nakhon Ratchasima lizard") is a genus of iguanodontian ornithopod dinosaur from Early Cretaceous (Aptian stage) Khok Kruat Formation of Nakhon Ratchasima Province in northeastern Thailand.

dinosaurs.name: Rativates
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rativates is a genus of ornithomimid theropod dinosaur from the Campanian-aged (Late Cretaceous) Dinosaur Park Formation of Alberta, Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Rebbachisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rebbachisaurus (meaning "Aït Rebbach lizard") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur of the superfamily Diplodocoidea, that lived during the Late Cretaceous period in Africa and possibly also South America about 99-97 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Regaliceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Regaliceratops (meaning "Royal horned face") is a monospecific genus of chasmosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur from Alberta, Canada that lived during the Late Cretaceous (middle Maastrichtian stage, 68.5 to 67.5 Ma) in what is now the St.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Regaliceratops skull at Royal Tyrrell Museum

dinosaurs.name: Regnosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Regnosaurus (meaning "Sussex lizard") is an extinct genus of herbivorous presumably armored dinosaur known from the early Cretaceous Wealden Formation of England.

dinosaurs.name: Rhabdodon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rhabdodon (meaning "fluted tooth") is a genus of ornithopod dinosaur that lived in Europe approximately 72-69 million years ago in the Late Cretaceous.[1] The genus contains a single species, R. priscus.

dinosaurs.name: Rhinorex
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rhinorex is a genus of kritosaurin hadrosaur from the Late Cretaceous Neslen Formation, in central Utah.

dinosaurs.name: Rhoetosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rhoetosaurus (meaning "Rhoetus lizard", after a titan in Greek mythology) is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic (Oxfordian)[1] Walloon Coal Measures of what is now eastern Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Richardoestesia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Richardoestesia is a morphogenus of theropod dinosaur teeth, originally described from the Late Cretaceous of what is now Canada, the United States, and possibly also Uzbekistan.

dinosaurs.name: Rinchenia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rinchenia (named after Byambyn Rinchen) is a genus of oviraptorid dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous epoch in what is now Mongolia, Nemegt Formation, around 70 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Rinconsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rinconsaurus is a genus of titanosaur sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous in what is now Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Riojasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Riojasaurus (meaning "lizard from La Rioja") was a herbivorous sauropodomorph dinosaur named after La Rioja Province in Argentina where it was found in the Los Colorados Formation in the Ischigualasto-Villa Unión Basin by José Bonaparte.

dinosaurs.name: Riojavenatrix
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Riojavenatrix (meaning "La Rioja huntress") is a genus of spinosaurid theropod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous Enciso Group of La Rioja, Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Riparovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Riparovenator ("riverbank hunter") is a genus of baryonychine spinosaurid dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Barremian) period of Britain.

dinosaurs.name: Rocasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rocasaurus (meaning "General Roca lizard") is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod that lived in South America.

dinosaurs.name: Ruehleia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ruehleia is a genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur from the Late Triassic period of Germany.

dinosaurs.name: Rugops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rugops (meaning 'wrinkle face') is a monospecific genus of basal abelisaurid theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period (Cenomanian stage, approximately 95 million years ago) in what is now the Echkar Formation in Niger.

dinosaurs.name: Rukwatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Rukwatitan is a genus of titanosaur sauropod dinosaur from the Galula Formation in Tanzania.

dinosaurs.name: Ruyangosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Ruyangosaurus (Ruyang County lizard) is a genus of titanosauriform sauropod dinosaur recovered from the Early Cretaceous Haoling Formation of China.

dinosaurs.name: Saichania
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Saichania (Mongolian meaning "beautiful one") is a genus of herbivorous ankylosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous period of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Saltasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Saltasaurus (which means "lizard from Salta") is a genus of saltasaurid dinosaur of the Late Cretaceous period of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Saltriovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Saltriovenator (meaning "Saltrio hunter") is a genus of ceratosaurian dinosaur that lived during the Sinemurian stage of the Early Jurassic in what is now Italy.

dinosaurs.name: Sanjuansaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sanjuansaurus ("San Juan Province lizard") is a genus of herrerasaurid dinosaur from the Late Triassic (Carnian) Ischigualasto Formation of the Ischigualasto-Villa Unión Basin in northwestern Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Sanpasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sanpasaurus ("Sanpa lizard") is a poorly known sauropod dinosaur from the Early Jurassic of Sichuan, China.

dinosaurs.name: Santanaraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Santanaraptor (meaning "Santana Formation thief") is a genus of coelurosaurian theropod dinosaur that lived in South America during the Early Cretaceous (late Aptian-early Albian), about 112 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Sanxiasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sanxiasaurus (meaning "Sanxia lizard", after the Three Gorges, Chinese Sanxia, of the Yangtze River) is a genus of neornithischian dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic Xintiangou Formation in the Chongqing Municipality of China.

dinosaurs.name: Sarahsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sarahsaurus is a genus of basal sauropodomorph dinosaur which lived during the Early Jurassic period in what is now northeastern Arizona, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Sarcolestes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sarcolestes (from Ancient Greek σάρξ (sárx), meaning "flesh", and λῃστής (lēistḗs), meaning "robber", and thus, "flesh robber") is an extinct genus of ankylosaurian ornithischian dinosaur from the Oxford Clay of England.

dinosaurs.name: Sarcosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sarcosaurus (Latin: "flesh lizard") is a genus of basal neotheropod dinosaur, roughly 3.5 metres (11 ft) long.

dinosaurs.name: Sarmientosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sarmientosaurus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur belonging to the Titanosauria.

dinosaurs.name: Sasayamagnomus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sasayamagnomus (meaning "gnome from Sasayama") is an extinct genus of neoceratopsian dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous (Albian age) Ohyamashimo Formation of Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan.

dinosaurs.name: Saurolophus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Saurolophus (; meaning "lizard crest") is a genus of large hadrosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period of North America and Asia, in what is now the Horseshoe Canyon and Nemegt formations respectively (about 70 to 66 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Sauroniops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sauroniops is a controversial genus of carnivorous carcharodontosaurid theropod dinosaurs known from the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian stage) of Morocco.

dinosaurs.name: Sauropelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sauropelta ( SOR-oh-PEL-tə; meaning 'lizard shield') is a genus of nodosaurid dinosaur that existed in the Early Cretaceous Period of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Saurophaganax
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Saurophaganax ("lord of lizard-eaters") is a dubious, chimeric genus of large saurischian dinosaur, possibly a sauropod, from the Late Jurassic (Kimmeridgian) Morrison Formation of Oklahoma, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Sauroposeidon
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sauroposeidon (/ˌsɔːroʊpoʊˈsaɪdən/ SOR-o-po-SY-dən; meaning "lizard earthquake god", after the Greek god Poseidon[3][4]) is a genus of sauropod dinosaur known from several incomplete specimens including a bone bed and fossilized trackways that have been found

dinosaurs.name: Saurornithoides
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Saurornithoides (/sɔːˌrɔːrnɪˈθɔɪdiːz/ saw-ROR-ni-THOY-deez) is a genus of troodontid maniraptoran dinosaur, which lived during the Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Saurornitholestes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Saurornitholestes ("lizard-bird thief") is a genus of carnivorous dromaeosaurid theropod dinosaur from the late Cretaceous of Canada (Alberta and Saskatchewan[3]) and the United States (Montana, New Mexico, Alabama, and South Carolina).

dinosaurs.name: Savannasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Savannasaurus is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Winton Formation of Queensland, Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Scansoriopteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Scansoriopteryx ("climbing wing") is a genus of maniraptoran dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Scelidosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Scelidosaurus (/ˌsɛlɪdoʊˈsɔːrəs/; with the intended meaning of "limb lizard", from Greek skelis/σκελίς meaning 'rib of beef' and sauros/σαυρος meaning 'lizard')[1] is a genus of herbivorous armoured ornithischian dinosaur from the Jurassic of England.

dinosaurs.name: Scipionyx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Scipionyx (/ˈʃɪˌpiːoʊnɪks, ˌʃɪpiːˈoʊ-/ SHIH-pee-oh-nicks-,[1] ship-ee-OH-[2]) was a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Pietraroja Formation of Italy, around 113 million years ago.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: 9121 - Milano, Museo storia naturale - Scipionyx samniticus - Foto Giovanni Dall'Orto 22-Apr-2007a

dinosaurs.name: Sciurumimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sciurumimus ("squirrel mimic," named for its tail's resemblance to that of the tree squirrel genus Sciurus[1]) is an extinct genus of tetanuran theropod from the Late Jurassic Torleite Formation of Germany.

dinosaurs.name: Scolosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Scolosaurus is an extinct genus of ankylosaurid dinosaurs within the subfamily Ankylosaurinae.

dinosaurs.name: Scutellosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Scutellosaurus (/skuːˌtɛloʊˈsɔːrəs/ skoo-TEL-oh-SOR-əs) is a genus of basal thyreophoran dinosaur that lived approximately 196 million years ago during the early part of the Jurassic Period in what is now Arizona, USA.

dinosaurs.name: Secernosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Secernosaurus (meaning "severed lizard") is a genus of herbivorous hadrosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous of what is now Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Segisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Segisaurus (meaning "Tsegi Canyon lizard") is a genus of small coelophysid theropod dinosaur, that measured approximately 1 metre (3.3 feet) in length.

dinosaurs.name: Segnosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Segnosaurus is a genus of therizinosaurid dinosaur that lived in what is now southeastern Mongolia during the Late Cretaceous, about 102–86 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Seitaad
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Seitaad is a genus of sauropodomorph dinosaur which lived during the Early Jurassic period in what is now southern Utah, United States.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Serikornis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Serikornis is a genus of small, feathered anchiornithid dinosaur from the Upper Jurassic Tiaojishan Formation of Liaoning, China.

dinosaurs.name: Shanag
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Shanag is a genus of paravian theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Period of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Shantungosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Shantungosaurus (lit.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: 山東龍骨架 在諸城

dinosaurs.name: Shaochilong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Shaochilong (meaning "shark toothed dragon") is an extinct genus of tetanuran theropod dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous (Barremian to Albian stages) Miaogou Formation of China, though a more restrictive suggestion from the Aptian to the Albian has been suggested based on faunal composition.

dinosaurs.name: Shenzhousaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Shenzhousaurus (meaning "Shenzhou lizard") is a genus of basal ornithomimosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Lower Cretaceous of China.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Shidaisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Shidaisaurus is a genus of metriacanthosaurid dinosaur.[1] Its fossil was found in early Middle Jurassic-age rocks of the Chuanjie Formation in Yunnan, China.

dinosaurs.name: Shishugounykus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Shishugounykus (meaning "Shishugou claw") is a genus of basal alvarezsaurian dinosaur from the Shishugou Formation of Xinjiang, China.

dinosaurs.name: Shri
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Shri (named after Palden Lhamo, a Buddhist deity) is an extinct genus of small dromaeosaurid dinosaurs that lived in what is now Mongolia during the Late Cretaceous.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Skeletal reconstruction of Shri rapax (transparent background)

dinosaurs.name: Shunosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Shunosaurus (lit.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Shunosaurus - Finnish Museum of Natural History

dinosaurs.name: Shuvuuia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Shuvuuia is a genus of bird-like theropod dinosaur from the late Cretaceous period of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Siamosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Siamosaurus (meaning "Siam lizard") is a potentially dubious genus of spinosaurid dinosaur that lived in what is now Thailand and possibly China during the Early Cretaceous period (Barremian to Aptian) and is the first reported spinosaurid from Asia.

dinosaurs.name: Siamotyrannus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Siamotyrannus (meaning "Siamese tyrant") is a genus of possibly allosauroid theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Sao Khua Formation of Thailand.

dinosaurs.name: Siats
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Siats (/see-ats/) is an extinct genus of large theropod dinosaurs known from the Late Cretaceous Cedar Mountain Formation (Mussentuchit Member) of Utah, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Sibirotitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sibirotitan ("Siberian titan") is a genus of somphospondyl sauropod from the Early Cretaceous (Barremian age) Ilek Formation of Russia.

dinosaurs.name: Sierraceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sierraceratops (meaning "Sierra horned face") is a genus of chasmosaurine ceratopsian from the Late Cretaceous Hall Lake Formation of New Mexico, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Sigilmassasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sigilmassasaurus ( see-jil-MAH-sə-SOR-əs; "Sijilmassa lizard") is a controversial genus of spinosaurid dinosaur that lived approximately 100 to 94 million years ago during the Late Cretaceous Period in what is now northern Africa.

dinosaurs.name: Silutitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Silutitan (meaning "Silk Road giant") is a genus of euhelopodid sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Shengjinkou Formation of Xinjiang, China.

dinosaurs.name: Silvisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Silvisaurus, from the Latin silva "woodland" and Greek sauros "lizard", is a nodosaurid ankylosaur from the Early to Late Cretaceous period.

dinosaurs.name: Similicaudipteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Similicaudipteryx, meaning "similar to Caudipteryx" (which itself means "tail feather"), is a genus of theropod dinosaur of the family Caudipteridae.

dinosaurs.name: Sinocalliopteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinocalliopteryx (meaning 'Chinese beautiful feather') is a genus of carnivorous coelurosaurian theropod dinosaurs from the Lower Cretaceous Yixian Formation of China (Jianshangou Beds, dating to 124.6 Ma).

dinosaurs.name: Sinoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinoceratops /ˌsaɪnoʊˈsɛrətɒps/ is an extinct genus of ceratopsian dinosaur that lived from 77.3 to 73.5 million years ago during the latter part of the Cretaceous Period in what is now Shandong province in China.

dinosaurs.name: Sinocoelurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinocoelurus (meaning "Chinese hollow tail", in reference to location and to relate the new genus to the North American Coelurus) is a genus of theropod dinosaur from the Oxfordian-?Tithonian-age Upper Jurassic Kyangyan Series of Sichuan, China.

dinosaurs.name: Sinornithoides
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinornithoides (meaning "Chinese bird form") is a genus of troodontid theropod dinosaurs that lived during the Early Cretaceous (Aptian or Albian stage, around 113 million years ago) of Inner Mongolia, China.[1] It contains only a single species, S. youngi.

dinosaurs.name: Sinornithomimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinornithomimus is a genus of ornithomimid dinosaur that lived in the Late Cretaceous Ulansuhai Formation of Alshanzuo Banner, Inner Mongolia, northern China.

dinosaurs.name: Sinornithosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinornithosaurus (derived from a combination of Latin and Greek, meaning 'Chinese bird-lizard') is a genus of feathered dromaeosaurid dinosaur from the early Cretaceous Period (late Barremian) of the Yixian Formation in what is now China.[1] It was the fifth non–avian feathered dinosaur genus discovered by 1999.

dinosaurs.name: Sinosauropteryx
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinosauropteryx (meaning "Chinese reptilian wing") is an extinct genus of coelurosaurian theropod dinosaurs.

dinosaurs.name: Sinosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinosaurus (meaning "Chinese lizard") is an extinct genus of basal theropod dinosaur which lived during the Early Jurassic (Hettangian-Sinemurian).

dinosaurs.name: Sinotyrannus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinotyrannus (meaning "Chinese tyrant") is an extinct genus of large tyrannosauroid theropod dinosaurs known from the Early Cretaceous Jiufotang Formation of Liaoning, China.

dinosaurs.name: Sinovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinovenator (meaning "Chinese hunter") is a genus of troodontid dinosaur from China.

dinosaurs.name: Sinraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinraptor (/sɪnˈræptər/) is a genus of metriacanthosaurid theropod dinosaur from the Shishugou Formation China.

dinosaurs.name: Sinusonasus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sinusonasus is a genus of dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous Period, recovered from the Yixian Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Sirindhorna
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sirindhorna is a genus of hadrosauroid ornithopod dinosaur from Early Cretaceous deposits of northeastern Thailand.[1]

dinosaurs.name: Skorpiovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Skorpiovenator ("scorpion hunter") is a genus of abelisaurid theropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian to Turonian) Huincul Formation of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Smitanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Smitanosaurus (meaning "smith lizard") is a genus of dicraeosaurid sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic of Colorado.

dinosaurs.name: Sonidosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sonidosaurus (meaning "Sonid lizard", after Sonid, the large geographical area that includes the type locality[2]) is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Sonorasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sonorasaurus is a genus of brachiosaurid sauropod dinosaur from the Early to Late Cretaceous (Albian to Cenomanian stages, around 112 to 93 million years ago).

dinosaurs.name: Soriatitan
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Soriatitan ("Soria titan") is a genus of brachiosaurid sauropod from the Early Cretaceous of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Spectrovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Spectrovenator ("ghost hunter") is a genus of basal abelisaurid theropod dinosaur that lived during the early Cretaceous period in what is now Brazil.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Spectrovenator ragei skeletal reconstruction by Felipe Elias

dinosaurs.name: Sphaerotholus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Sphaerotholus is a genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaur from the Upper Cretaceous of the western United States and Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Spicomellus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Spicomellus is an extinct genus of unusual early ankylosaurian dinosaur from the El Mers III Formation (Bathonian age) of Morocco.

dinosaurs.name: Spinophorosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Spinophorosaurus is a genus of sauropod dinosaur that lived in what is now Niger during the Middle Jurassic period.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Spinophorosaurus nigerensis - Naturhistorisches Museum, Braunschweig, Germany - DSC05276

dinosaurs.name: Spinops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Spinops is an extinct genus of centrosaurine ceratopsian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Alberta, southern Canada.

dinosaurs.name: Spinosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Spinosaurus (; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Spinostropheus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Spinostropheus is a genus of theropod dinosaur that lived in the Middle Jurassic period and has been found in the Tiourarén Formation, Niger.

dinosaurs.name: Staurikosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Staurikosaurus (Pronounced /ˌstɔɹ̠ikoʊˈsɔɹ̠ʌs/, STOR-ree-koh-SOR-ruhs; "Southern Cross lizard") is a genus of herrerasaurid dinosaur from the Late Triassic of Brazil, found in the Santa Maria Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Stegoceras
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stegoceras is a genus of pachycephalosaurid (dome-headed) dinosaur that lived in what is now North America during the Late Cretaceous period, about 77.5 to 74 million years ago (mya).

dinosaurs.name: Stegopelta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stegopelta (meaning "roofed shield") is a genus of struthiosaurin nodosaurid dinosaur based on a partial skeleton from the Cretaceous (latest Albian-earliest Cenomanian) Belle Fourche Member of the Frontier Formation of Fremont County, Wyoming, USA.

dinosaurs.name: Stegosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stegosaurus (; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Stegouros
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stegouros (, meaning "roofed tail") is an extinct genus of ankylosaurian dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous Dorotea Formation of southern Chile.

dinosaurs.name: Stellasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stellasaurus (meaning "star lizard"; both in reference to the shape of its head ornamentation and as an homage to the song "Starman" by David Bowie) is a genus of centrosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur that lived in Montana during the Late Cretaceous.

dinosaurs.name: Stenonychosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stenonychosaurus (meaning "narrow claw lizard") is a disputed genus of troodontid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous Dinosaur Park Formation of Alberta, Canada, as well as possibly the Two Medicine Formation.

dinosaurs.name: Stenopelix
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stenopelix (meaning "narrow pelvis") is a genus of basal ceratopsian from the Early Cretaceous (late Berriasian stage, approximately 140 million years ago) of Germany.

dinosaurs.name: Stephanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stephanosaurus (meaning "crown lizard") is a dubious genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur with a complicated taxonomic history.

dinosaurs.name: Stokesosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stokesosaurus (meaning "Stokes' lizard") is a genus of early tyrannosauroid theropod dinosaurs from the late Jurassic period of Utah, United States.

dinosaurs.name: Streptospondylus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Streptospondylus, from Ancient Greek στρεπτός (streptós), meaning "twisted", and σπόνδυλος (spóndulos), meaning "vertebra", is a genus of tetanuran theropod dinosaur known from the Late Jurassic period of France, 161 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Struthiomimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Struthiomimus (meaning "ostrich mimic", from the Ancient Greek στρούθειος/stroutheios, meaning "of the ostrich", and μῖμος/mimos, meaning "mimic" or "imitator") is a genus of ornithomimid dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous of what is now western North America.

dinosaurs.name: Struthiosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Struthiosaurus (Latin struthio = ostrich + Greek sauros = lizard) is a genus of nodosaurid dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous period (Santonian-Maastrichtian) of Austria, Romania, France, and possibly Hungary.[1][2] It was a small dinosaur, measuring 2–3 m (6.

dinosaurs.name: Stygivenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Stygivenator, also known as the "Jordan theropod" is a dubious genus of tyrannosauroid dinosaur from the Hell Creek Formation, containing the type and only species Stygivenator molnari.

dinosaurs.name: Styracosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Styracosaurus (/stɪˌrækəˈsɔːrəs/ sti-RAK-ə-SOR-əs; meaning "spiked lizard" from the Ancient Greek styrax/στύραξ "spike at the butt-end of a spear-shaft" and sauros/σαῦρος "lizard")[1] is an extinct genus of herbivorous ceratopsian dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian stage) of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Suchomimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Suchomimus is a genus of large theropod dinosaur that lived in what is now Niger during the Albian to Aptian stages of the Early Cretaceous period, 112 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Suchosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Suchosaurus (meaning "crocodile lizard") is a dubious genus of spinosaurid dinosaur from Cretaceous England and Portugal, originally believed to be a genus of crocodile.

dinosaurs.name: Supersaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Supersaurus (meaning "super lizard") is a genus of diplodocid sauropod dinosaur that lived in North America during the Late Jurassic period.

dinosaurs.name: Suskityrannus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Suskityrannus (meaning "coyote tyrant", suski meaning "coyote" in Zuni) is a genus of small tyrannosauroid theropod from the Late Cretaceous of southern Laramidia.

dinosaurs.name: Suzhousaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Suzhousaurus (meaning "Suzhou lizard") is an extinct genus of large therizinosaurid dinosaurs from the Early Cretaceous of China.

dinosaurs.name: Syngonosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Syngonosaurus is an extinct genus of ornithopod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Catalogue of the fossil Reptilia and Amphibia in the British Museum (Natural history) By Richard Lydekker (1888) (20571332452)

dinosaurs.name: Szechuanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Szechuanosaurus ("Szechuan lizard") is an extinct genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Tachiraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tachiraptor is a genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaurs found in the early Jurassic period La Quinta Formation of Venezuela.

dinosaurs.name: Talarurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Talarurus (/ˌtæləˈrʊərəs/ TAL-ə-ROOR-əs; meaning "basket tail" or "wicker tail") is a genus of ankylosaurid dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Late Cretaceous period, about 96 million to 89 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Taleta
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Taleta (meaning "three") is an extinct genus of lambeosaurine ornithopod dinosaurs from the Late Cretaceous (Maastrichtian age) Oulad Abdoun Basin of Morocco.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Taleta jaw lateral by Nick Longrich

dinosaurs.name: Tamarro
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tamarro (named after a mythological creature in local culture) is a genus of troodontid theropod from the Late Cretaceous Talarn Formation (Tremp Group) of Spain.

dinosaurs.name: Tambatitanis
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tambatitanis (meaning "Tamba giant", after Tamba, the name given to the northwest of Kansai, Japan[1]) is an extinct genus of titanosauriform, possibly titanosaurian, sauropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous-aged (early Albian) Ohyamashimo Formation of the Sasayama Group.

dinosaurs.name: Tameryraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tameryraptor ("thief from the beloved land") is an extinct genus of large carcharodontosaurid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous (Cenomanian age) in what is now Egypt.

dinosaurs.name: Tangvayosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tangvayosaurus (meaning "Tang Vay lizard") is a genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Lower Cretaceous (Aptian to Albian ages) Grès supérieurs Formation of Savannakhet Province, Laos.

dinosaurs.name: Tanius
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tanius (meaning "of Tan") is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Tanycolagreus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tanycolagreus is a genus of coelurosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic (Kimmeridgian to Tithonian) of the U.S. states of Wyoming, Utah, and Colorado.

dinosaurs.name: Tapuiasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tapuiasaurus (meaning "Tapuia lizard") is a genus of titanosaur which lived during the Lower Cretaceous period (Aptian age) in what is now Minas Gerais, Brazil.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Museum of Zoology of the University of São Paulo 2018 25

dinosaurs.name: Tarascosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tarascosaurus ("Tarasque lizard") is a genus of abelisaurid theropod dinosaur from Late Cretaceous of France.

dinosaurs.name: Tarbosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tarbosaurus ( TAR-bə-SOR-əs; meaning "alarming lizard") is a genus of large tyrannosaurid dinosaur that lived in Asia during the Maastrichtian age of the Late Cretaceous epoch.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Tarbosaurus bataar mount - Dinosaur Mysteries exhibit

dinosaurs.name: Tarchia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tarchia (meaning "brainy one") is a genus of herbivorous ankylosaurid dinosaur from the late Cretaceous of Mongolia.

dinosaurs.name: Tastavinsaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tastavinsaurus is a genus of sauropod dinosaur belonging to the Titanosauriformes.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Tastavinsaurus - El Castellar, Teruel, Spain - Left femur, tibia & fibula

dinosaurs.name: Tatankaceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tatankaceratops (meaning "bison horned face") is a controversial genus of herbivorous chasmosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur that lived during the Late Cretaceous period (latest Maastrichtian stage, about 66 million years ago) in what is now the upper Hell Creek Formation, in South Dakota.

dinosaurs.name: Tatisaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tatisaurus is a genus of ornithischian dinosaur from the Early Jurassic from the Lower Lufeng Formation in Yunnan Province in China.

dinosaurs.name: Taurovenator
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Taurovenator, from Latin taurus, meaning "bull", and venator, meaning "hunter", is an extinct genus of large, probable carcharodontosaurid theropod dinosaurs that lived in what is now Argentina during the Cenomanian age of the Late Cretaceous, around 95 to 93.9 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Tazoudasaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tazoudasaurus is a genus of gravisaurian (probably vulcanodontid) sauropod dinosaurs from the late Early Jurassic (Toarcian).

dinosaurs.name: Tehuelchesaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tehuelchesaurus  is a genus of macronarian sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic (Oxfordian to Tithonian) of Argentina.

dinosaurs.name: Teinurosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Teinurosaurus is a genus of carnivorous theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Jurassic in what is now France.

dinosaurs.name: Telmatosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Telmatosaurus (meaning "marsh lizard") is a genus of basal hadrosauromorph dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Romania.

dinosaurs.name: Tendaguria
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tendaguria ( TEN-də-GURE-ee-ə; meaning "the Tendaguru one") is a genus of herbivorous sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic of Lindi Region, Tanzania.

dinosaurs.name: Tenontosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tenontosaurus (/tɪˌnɒntəˈsɔːrəs/ ti-NON-tə-SOR-əs; lit.

dinosaurs.name: Teratophoneus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Teratophoneus ("monstrous murderer"; Greek: teras, "monster" and phoneus, "murderer") is a genus of tyrannosaurine theropod dinosaur that lived during the late Campanian age of the Late Cretaceous period, (about 76.5 to 75.5 million years ago) in what is now Utah.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Teratophoneus curriei adult and juvenile 2 salt lake city

dinosaurs.name: Terminocavus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Terminocavus is a genus of ceratopsid dinosaur from the late Cretaceous Period of what is now North America.

dinosaurs.name: Tethyshadros
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tethyshadros ("Tethyan hadrosauroid") is a genus of hadrosauroid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous (Campanian) Calcare di Aurisina (previously thought to come from the younger Liburnia Formation) of Trieste, Italy.

dinosaurs.name: Texacephale
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Texacephale is a possibly dubious genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaur from the Campanian stage of the Late Cretaceous.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Texacephale langstoni skull dome by Nick Longrich

dinosaurs.name: Texasetes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Texasetes (meaning "Texas resident") is a genus of ankylosaurian dinosaurs from the late Lower Cretaceous of North America.

dinosaurs.name: Thanatotheristes
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
facts: Lived: Late Cretaceous, middle Campanian, 80.3-79.5 Ma
dinosaurs.description: Thanatotheristes (meaning "reaper of death" according to the original authors;[1] literally, "death harvester" in Greek: Θάνατος, romanized: Thánatos, "Death"[3] and θεριστής, romanized: theristḗs, "harvester"[4]) is a genus of tyrannosaurid dinosaur from the Late Cretaceous of Laramidia, approximately 80.1-79.5 Ma.

dinosaurs.name: Thanos
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Thanos (named for the Marvel Comics character) is a genus of carnivorous brachyrostran abelisaurid dinosaur that lived in Brazil during the Santonian stage of the late Cretaceous Period.

dinosaurs.name: Tharosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tharosaurus (meaning "Thar Desert lizard") is an extinct genus of potentially dubious eusauropod sauropod dinosaurs from the Middle Jurassic Jaisalmer Formation of India.

dinosaurs.name: Thecocoelurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Thecocoelurus is a genus of theropod dinosaur from the early Cretaceous period of England.

dinosaurs.name: Thecodontosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Thecodontosaurus ("socket-tooth lizard") is a genus of herbivorous basal sauropodomorph dinosaur that lived during the late Triassic period (Carnian age).[2] Its remains are known mostly from Triassic "fissure fillings" in southern England.

dinosaurs.name: Theiophytalia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Theiophytalia is a genus of herbivorous iguanodontian dinosaur from the lower Cretaceous period (Aptian-Albian stage, about 112 million years ago[1]) of Colorado, USA.

dinosaurs.name: Therizinosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Therizinosaurus (/ˌθɛrəˌzɪnoʊˈsɔːrəs/ ⓘ; meaning 'scythe lizard') is a genus of very large therizinosaurid dinosaurs that lived during the Late Cretaceous period in what is now Asia.

dinosaurs.name: Thespesius
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Thespesius (meaning "wondrous one") is a dubious genus of hadrosaurid dinosaur from the late Maastrichtian-age Upper Cretaceous Lance Formation of South Dakota.

dinosaurs.name: Thyreosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Thyreosaurus is an extinct genus of stegosaurian dinosaur from the Middle Jurassic El Mers III Formation of Morocco.

dinosaurs.name: Tianyulong
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tianyulong (Chinese: 天宇龍; Pinyin: tiānyǔlóng; named for the Shandong Tianyu Museum of Nature where the holotype fossil is housed) is an extinct genus of heterodontosaurid ornithischian dinosaur.

dinosaurs.name: Tianyuraptor
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tianyuraptor is a genus of short-armed dromaeosaurid dinosaur ('running lizard'; a type of small dinosaur considered to be closely related to birds) that lived during the Early Cretaceous, about 122 million years ago.

dinosaurs.name: Tianzhenosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tianzhenosaurus (meaning “Tianzhen lizard”) is a genus of ankylosaurid dinosaurs known from the Late Cretaceous Huiquanpu Formation of Shanxi Province, China.

dinosaurs.name: Tichosteus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tichosteus (meaning "walled bone") is a genus of herbivorous dinosaur from the Late Jurassic.

dinosaurs.name: Tienshanosaurus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tienshanosaurus (meaning "Tienshan lizard") is an extinct genus of sauropod dinosaur from the Late Jurassic of what is now China.

dinosaurs.name: Tietasaura
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Tietasaura (meaning "Tieta lizard") is an extinct genus of elasmarian ornithopod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous Marfim Formation of Brazil.

dinosaurs.name: Timimus
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Timimus is a genus of small coelurosaurian theropod dinosaur from the Early Cretaceous of Australia.

dinosaurs.name: Timurlengia
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Timurlengia is an extinct genus of tyrannosauroid dinosaurs known from the early Late Cretaceous (Turonian age) Bissekty Formation of the Kyzylkum Desert in Uzbekistan.

dinosaurs.name: Titanoceratops
dinosaurs.imageDescription: Curated full-body dinosaur image from DinosaurPictures.org.
dinosaurs.description: Titanoceratops (meaning "titanic horned face") is a controversial genus of chasmosaurine ceratopsian dinosaur that lived in the Late Cretaceous period (Campanian stage, about 75 million years ago[1]) in what is now New Mexico.

source.originalWikimediaTitle: Titanoceratops ouranos skull by Nick Longrich

dinosaurs.name: Titanomachya
